record.instruction: If you are a doctor, please answer the medical questions based on the patient's description.
record.input: Doctor, I think I might have a thoracic aortic aneurysm, can you please tell me what tests I need to do?Â
record.output: You will need to undergo a radiographic imaging procedure along with an electrocardiogram to diagnose a thoracic aortic aneurysm. Additionally, you will also need to do hematologic tests, a complete blood count, X-ray computed tomography, glucose measurement to check your glucose level, and kidney function tests to check your kidney function.

record.instruction: If you are a doctor, please answer the medical questions based on the patient's description.
record.input: Hi Doctor, I have an open wound on my arm due to a recent injury. The pain is quite severe and I am worried about proper treatment for it.
record.output: Hello, I am sorry to hear that. Based on your symptoms, I suggest we perform a complete physical skin exam along with wound care management. We will also need to suture the wound and examine your foot. Additionally, we need to do a CAT scan of your head to check for any possible head injury. We will also need to apply a splint to immobilize the injured limb. Itâ€™s important we also check your blood alcohol level to ensure proper wound care management.

record.instruction: If you are a doctor, please answer the medical questions based on the patient's description.
record.input: Doctor, I have these painful and recurring abscesses in my armpits and groin. They are causing a lot of discomfort and even when they subside, they leave behind scars. Iâ€™m wondering what tests I need to have done.
record.output: Based on your symptoms, it seems that you may be suffering from a condition called Hidradenitis Suppurativa. To confirm the diagnosis and determine the extent of the condition, we will need to carry out a complete physical skin exam performed by a dermatologist. We may need wound care management, examine your breasts and carry out an excision (removal) or incision and drainage (I&D) if required. We may also need to do a rectal examination and culture wound to determine the severity of the condition.

record.instruction: If you are a doctor, please answer the medical questions based on the patient's description.
record.input: Doctor, Iâ€™ve been experiencing hallucinations, difficulty sleeping, and have noticed changes in my ability to think and reason. My family has also mentioned that my movements seem slower and more uncoordinated. What could be wrong with me?
record.output: Your symptoms sound concerning for Lewy body dementia. In order to properly diagnose you, I will need to order a few medical tests. We will need to conduct a radiographic imaging procedure, including a CT scan of your head, as well as other diagnostic procedures including an interview, evaluation, and consultation. Additionally, we will need to measure your cardiac enzymes, take a blood culture, and perform cardiac monitoring to assess your heart function.

record.instruction: If you are a doctor, please answer the medical questions based on the patient's description.
record.input: Hi doctor, Iâ€™ve been experiencing fatigue and dizziness recently. I went to a different clinic and they diagnosed me with sinus bradycardia. What do I need to do next?
record.output: Sorry to hear that. Based on your symptoms and diagnosis, I suggest we perform an Electrocardiogram (ECG) to evaluate the electrical activity of your heart. We will also need to do some Hematologic tests and a Complete Blood Count (CBC) to check for any potential underlying causes. In addition, we may need to perform a Radiographic imaging procedure and a Plain X-ray (X-ray) to assess the extent of any structural changes that may have occurred. Lastly, we will need to run a glucose measurement (glucose level) and kidney function tests to rule out any concomitant metabolic conditions that could be contributing to your symptoms.

record.instruction: If you are a doctor, please answer the medical questions based on the patient's description.
record.input: Doctor, I have been experiencing severe joint pain and swelling. It hurts to even move my hand. What could be wrong?
record.output: Based on your symptoms, itâ€™s possible that you have septic arthritis. In order to confirm this, we need to run a few tests. We will need to perform Hematologic tests including Complete blood count (Cbc), Radiographic imaging procedure, specifically a Plain x-ray (X ray). Additionally, we might need to evaluate your Kidney function and Electrolytes panel as well. In the meantime, we will also begin Intravenous fluid replacement to ensure you are properly hydrated.

record.instruction: If you are a doctor, please answer the medical questions based on the patient's description.
record.input: Doctor, I have been experiencing some discomfort and sores in my genital area. I think it might be Granuloma inguinale, can you help me with this?
record.output: Thank you for sharing your symptoms with me. Yes, it is important to determine if itâ€™s Granuloma inguinale through medical tests. You need to go for a pelvis exam and a urinalysis. We may also need to check your CBC (Complete Blood Count) and conduct some hematologic tests (Blood Test). record.input: Hello doctor,I had mumps five months ago and after that, I started to have an infection in my left testes. It was swollen and now it has shrunk to almost half the size of the other one. As I am sexually active, I feel a pain in each of the vas deferens after sex. If I do not have sex for days, they become sensitive. I was treated with Ceftum 500 mg, the first time I had an infection. Now my question is, is there any chance that the infection is still in my body? And, do I need to get examined for it? For the time being, please suggest some precautionary antibiotics for my relief.
record.answer_icliniq: Hello, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. I can understand your concern. You had mumps and this is a viral infection known to cause an inflammation of the testis in some cases. Take care. For more information consult a sexologist online
record.answer_chatgpt: It is possible that you still have an infection in your testes, especially if you have been experiencing pain and sensitivity in your vas deferens. It is recommended that you consult with a doctor or urologist for an examination and further testing to determine if there is an ongoing infection. As an AI language model, I cannot prescribe medications or antibiotics. However, taking antibiotics without a doctor's prescription is not recommended. It is important to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1) There is no need to worry about reinfection as the infection caused by Mumps virus does not persist in the body and is completely cleared up by the immune system.2) The swelling at your scrotum is due to inflammation of epididymis or vas deferens which is a result of mumps infection.3) There is no need to take antibiotics for this problem. You can take anti-inflammatory medicines like Ibuprofen for symptomatic relief.4) Doing cool compresses over the scrotal area will help in reducing the inflammation and pain.

In case he is able to pick up any change or ChatDoctor. 4. Urgently visit your doctor in case of a sudden

Myopia or nearsightedness is a common vision problem where distant objects appear blurry. It's usually corrected with eyeglasses or contact lenses. Here are a few tips to help your son take care of his eyes: 1. Regular eye check-ups: Make sure your son gets regular eye check-ups so that any changes in his vision can be detected early. 2. Proper eyewear: Ensure that your son wears the appropriate eyewear, such as eyeglasses or contact lenses, to correct his vision. Encourage him to wear protective eyewear when participating in sports or other activities that could cause an injury to his eyes. 3. Good lighting: Provide good lighting when reading or doing homework to prevent eye strain. 4. Eye exercises: Encourage your son to do eye exercises that help strengthen and relax his eyes. 5. Healthy diet: Help your son maintain a healthy diet, which includes foods that are good for the eyes, such as fruits and vegetables. Remember, it's important to consult your optometrist or ophthalmologist for specific advice on how to take care of your son's eyes.
record.answer_chatdoctor: Hi, welcome to Chat Doctor forum. I understand your concern about your sons eyes. The best thing for you to do is to get an appointment with an ophthalmologist who can examine your sons eyes more thoroughly and determine if there are any treatable conditions that could be causing the vision loss in his eyes. There are some conditions that can cause vision loss at younger ages and it would not be wise to wait too long before getting him examined by a specialist. Bases on the available data, those who are 60 above, and with medical condition such as hypertension, cardiovascular disease and diabetes are the most vulnerable population. Thyroid disease and any other autommine condition is not known to be associated with increased risk of viral infections in general, nor is there an association between these disease and severity of the viral infection However, if you mild symptoms of cough and fever, I would still recommend that you do self quarantine and monitoring. I would still recommend that you wash your hands regularly with alcohol, soap and water and practice social distancing. I hope i wa able to answer your concern. Is there anything else I can help you with?

It may also be important to consult with your healthcare provider to determine if any specific recommendations or precautions are necessary for your individual situation.

record.input: Hello doctor,I would like to ask what is the likelihood that my partner who performs oral sex on me has trench mouth or acute necrotizing ulcerative gingivitis? He has no bad breath and he was able to have normal food. Can people with trench mouth have no bad breath and is able to eat normal food?
record.answer_icliniq: Hello. I understand your concern. Trench mouth or acute necrotizing ulcerative gingivitis (ANUG) is a bacterial infection and is uncommon nowadays but it is highly associated with HIV infection and in kids with nutritional deficiencies. To be diagnosed with trench mouth or ANUG, symptoms like gum necrosis and ulceration, pain and bleeding (on just touching the gums) should be present. The main characteristic feature is fetid or the putrid odor which is caused due to the rotting gum tissue. And people diagnosed with ANUG has this odor, unless if they are under treatment in a very initial stage. It is certainly difficult for people with ANUG to eat a portion of normal or even less spicy food if they are not under treatment. It is treatable within a week by the usual antibiotic regimen and medicated mouth rinses. Gum infection such as this cannot occur only by fellatio. If your partner is diagnosed with immunocompromised diseases, respiratory infection, poor nutrition or has the habit of smoking, practice caution during the act. Proper protection and oral hygiene practices will prevent such infections. I hope this helps. Take care.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot diagnose medical conditions, and it is always best to consult a qualified healthcare provider who can assess and treat any oral health issues. However, in general, trench mouth or acute necrotizing ulcerative gingivitis is a bacterial infection that affects the gums and can cause severe pain, bad breath, and difficulty eating. While it is transmitted through contact with contaminated saliva and can be transferred during oral sex, it is not uncommon for people with the condition to have no symptoms or minimal symptoms. It is important to practice good oral hygiene, including brushing and flossing regularly, and to see a dentist or healthcare provider if you have any concerns about your partner's or your own oral health.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1) your boyfriend had no gum bleeding, so it is unlikely that he has Trenchmouth (necrotizing ulcerative gingivitis).2) The symptoms of Trenchmouth are gumbleeding, pain, swelling in the gums, difficulty in eating and talking, etc.3) People with Trenchmouth may not be able to eat normal food because of severe pain.4) But if your boyfriend does not have any other symptom than he can be treated for gingivitis only.5) You should visit a dentist and get him examined.6) He will need scaling and polishing of his teeth and gums. Hope I have answered your query. Let me know if I can assist you further.

record.input: Hello doctor,Attached is a treadmill stress test result. I would like to obtain technical info regarding these results, particularly regarding the infarction. I would appreciate the severity of this infarction and whether it could be a machine error. Could you also tell me by perhaps drawing on the shot what waves and shapes indicate problems?
record.answer_icliniq: Hello, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. I have gone through your report (attachment removed to protect patient identity). It is unlikely to be a machine error. Errors do not have such patterns and it is suggestive of old infarction only. Its present in inferior leads, which suggests inferior part of the heart is damaged and these are relatively less severe kind of infarction. However, an echo will give a better idea regarding the severity and extent of damage done. So it is better to get an echo done, if not done yet. Now, you can see in lead 1, P wave followed by a sharp spike called as QRS complex followed by an upright wave called as T-wave. So now, you can see in lead 2, 3, and AVF which are inferior leads, showing downward QRS spike instead of upward and T-wave is also inverted suggestive of heart damage. How much worried you should be about his tonsil and sleep apnea problem are: 1. Change in facial features producing adenoid facies. 2. Irritability of kid. 3. He cannot hear properly due to adenoids blocking ear tubes. 4. Ear discharge. 5. Reduced development of speech. Do not get worried just because of the snoring and sleep apnea. Give him nasal steroids and antihistaminic syrup (like Zyrtec or Allegra syrup) of appropriate dosage. In some cases, we even give oral steroid syrup. It does not cause any stunting the way you have understood. Once you exhaust the above medical treatment option and find no or not much benefit we can discuss about the surgery. Your doctor is right in giving the medicines Flonase, etc. That is the protocol. We can follow the same here. The decision to operate may vary with different surgeon. record.input: Hi doctor,I had unprotected sex on day 13 of my cycle and had an I-pill a day later. Again, I had sex in a gap of three days after the intake of tablet. I am really worried if I should take the pill again. This was a terrible mistake. Please help.
record.answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. I am assuming that you have regular monthly periods. In that case, it is the time where you are going to ovulate. So, it is an unsafe period to have sex. And, if you have once unprotected sex and have taken I-pill (emergency contraceptive pill) once, then why to make mistake for the second time. Because, I-pill in itself is not 100% effective even if taken once. There is no study about two times taken I-pill. It works with two mechanisms. First of all, it will delay ovulation and second, it brings bleeding. So, if pregnancy happens, it resists implantation. In your case, if I believe that ovulation is delayed while you have taken the tablet, then it is good. If you have taken the tablet for the second time, then the ovulation is more delayed and period will be somewhat late with more chance of avoiding pregnancy. The second thing is that with I-pill your period cycle can become irregular. The chance of that increases more if you have taken two times. Because, it is a chemical (hormone), which is produced in your body to maintain your regular cycle. And, when you take it from outside it can disturb your normal cycle. If you do not want pregnancy in the first place, then either use condom or other method as preferred by you both after discussing with your doctor. I-pill is an emergency contraceptive and not a regular contraceptive pill that you can take as and when required. It should be taken when fear of failure of normal contraception is more. So, make the thing clear about whether you want pregnancy or not and go for a solution which helps you in future.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I'm not authorized to prescribe medication nor diagnose any conditions. However, I can provide you with some general information. The emergency contraceptive pill is meant to be used as a backup method of contraception and should not be used as a regular method. Also, it does not provide protection against sexually transmitted infections (STIs). It is recommended that you use a regular contraceptive method to prevent unintended pregnancy. If you have concerns regarding your current contraceptive methods or are not using any, I would advise you to consult a healthcare provider. In addition, if you are worried about the possibility of pregnancy after the recent sexual encounter, you could consider taking a pregnancy test. It is advisable to take the test after the expected date of your period. Again, I would strongly encourage you to seek advice from a healthcare professional to discuss your options and develop a plan for safe and effective contraception.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1) You did not mention what kind of pill you took.2) The only thing that is important is the timing of the pill relative to your ovulation date or the time when you would have expected to ovulate. If the pill was taken prior to the release of your egg/ovulation then it will prevent pregnancy.3) But if you were already past the point of ovulating then there is no reason for you to worry as long as you do not become pregnant within the next 72 hours.4) The other thing to be concerned about is having any side effects from the pill like nausea, vomiting, breast tenderness etc. These symptoms usually start about 5-6 days after taking the pill.5) There is no reason to repeat the same mistake again. Be more careful next time around.

One of the popular EC pills available in the market is "I-pill". The procedure to take the I-pill is as follows: 1. Take the pill as soon as possible after unsafe sex. 2. The common cold is caused by a viral infection in the upper respiratory tract. There are many different types of cold viruses, so it's possible to catch a cold multiple times in a year. If you do catch a cold, it's important to rest, drink plenty of fluids, and avoid spreading the virus to others by covering your mouth and nose when you cough or sneeze.

record.input: Hello doctor,My fiancee is B positive and myself O negative. In the future, will there be any complications in my pregnancy?
record.answer_icliniq: Hello, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. There would be no complications during the first pregnancy but may occur in the subsequent ones due to isoimmunization phenomenon. It is a problem which arises if the mother is having negative blood group and baby is having positive blood group. But treatments are available, so you have nothing to worry about it. I hope it has helped you. If you require any further queries, please do not hesitate to contact me.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot provide medical advice, so it's best to speak with your doctor or a genetic counselor regarding any concerns. However, I can share basic information regarding blood types and pregnancy. If you are Rh-negative and your partner is Rh-positive, there is a risk that your baby could inherit the Rh-positive factor from your partner. This could cause your body to react as if the Rh-positive factor is foreign, potentially leading to Rh sensitization, a condition in which your body produces antibodies to attack the Rh-positive blood cells. This could cause complications in any future pregnancies, including hemolytic disease of the newborn (HDN), in which these antibodies attack and destroy the fetus's red blood cells. However, there is a treatment to prevent Rh sensitization called Rh immunoglobulin (RhIg) that can be given during pregnancy and after childbirth to prevent complications. It's important to speak with your doctor and have regular prenatal care to monitor any potential complications and receive appropriate treatment.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1) B positive blood group is not a risk factor for pregnancy.2) You can have a safe pregnancy if your partner is B positive.3) The antibodies that cause Rh (Rhesus) sensitization are produced by your own body during pregnancy.4) All women who are Rh negative should receive an injection of anti-D immunoglobulin when they first become pregnant, ideally within 72 hours after delivery.5) After receiving this injection, all babies born to these mothers will be Rh negative and protected from developing Rh disease.6) It is important for your partner to receive the above-mentioned injection before conception as well.

record.input: Hello doctor,I understand how bizarre my question can sound, given the circumstances, but still, it is an urgent matter for me. I was staying in a hotel and masturbated in the bathroom (in my room). I fear that I could have obtained HIV, if some semen or blood of the previous visitor had been on the facilities (or on the soap, which was dry and in a box and was not exposed to fresh air or light) and then came in contact with my penis (or some small wounds on penis or hands, if there were some, I do not remember their presence though). Can you look at it seriously and answer (with explanation), what is the probability for me to have obtained HIV? Even small real probability frightens me. I am a virgin.
record.answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. There is absolutely no chance of acquiring HIV this way. HIV is only transmitted through unprotected sex e.g., either vaginal, anal or oral sex. Firstly, the virus does not survive outside the body for more than a few seconds and dies quickly in ChatDoctor. Secondly, intact skin does not provide access to the bloodstream. However, even if we assume that you had a break in your skin barrier at that time, then also a paper thin cut or a scratch that is not even bleeding does not provide access to the bloodstream. There has to be a fresh actively bleeding deep cut for the virus to gain access to the bloodstream. In my view, you have no risk of HIV, whatsoever. For more information consult an HIV AIDS specialist online
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I don't judge or discriminate. It can be difficult to confront risks that are potentially terrifying, and its great that you're reaching out. However, I can provide you with the facts. First of all, it's highly unlikely that you would acquire HIV in the manner described. HIV is primarily transmitted through sexual contact, blood to blood contact, mother to child during childbirth or breastfeeding, and the sharing of needles or other injection drug equipment that's come into contact with infected blood. It's extremely unlikely that any blood or bodily fluids from a previous hotel guest would have been present on a clean towel, a clean bar of soap, or any other bathroom fixtures. Additionally, HIV needs direct access to your bloodstream to infect you. It can't penetrate intact skin, so unless you had fresh cuts, sores, or other openings in your genital area or hands, it's even less likely for HIV to spread through such a scenario (and oftentimes the viral concentration in blood is at levels too low for infection to take place). To sum it up: while any sexually active individual should always take precautions (such as condom usage) to reduce the risk of HIV transmission, there's virtually no chance that you could acquire HIV in the way you described.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1st-HIV does NOT spread through water, soap, or any other object like that.2nd-Most of your concerns are baseless because you are a virgin and never had sex before(even once).3rd-You have no risk of getting HIV from this incident.4th-HIV can only be transmitted through unprotected sexual intercourse, use of contaminated needles/blood transfusion.5th-HIV is very difficult to transmit even through sexual intercourse. So don't worry about it.

record.input: Hello doctor,I have applied steroid cream (Betnovate-C) and my skin has become extremely thin and dark now. A dermatologist recommended me to try dermaroller so I want to know is it safe? And will it work? Please explain in detail as nothing has worked for me.
record.answer_icliniq: Hello. Indiscriminate use of topical steroids can cause skin thinning (due to collagen degradation) amongst other side effects like acne, facial hair, etc. Most of these side effects resolve gradually over next few months after stopping the topical steroid cream. This recovery can be aided by certain creams as well as certain procedures like dermaroller that help in collagen regeneration. Dermaroller is safe. The extent of improvement would depend on the extent of initial skin damage and the number of sessions. Moreover, results are individualized. For more information consult a dermatologist online Take care.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot give medical advice. However, I can provide general information regarding the safety and effectiveness of dermarollers. Dermarollers are medical devices that are used to treat various skin conditions, such as acne scars, wrinkles, and hyperpigmentation. They consist of a roller with numerous tiny needles that are used to puncture the skin's surface. This process stimulates the production of collagen, which helps to improve the skin's texture and overall appearance. While dermarollers can be effective, they are not without risks. The most common side effects of dermarolling include redness, swelling, and bruising. In some cases, there may be more serious side effects, such as infection or scarring. If you are considering using a dermaroller, it is important to consult a dermatologist or other qualified medical professional. They can help you determine if it is safe and appropriate for your specific skin type and condition. They can also provide guidance on how to use the device properly and minimize the risk of side effects. Additionally, it is important to understand that dermarollers may not work for everyone. While they can be effective for some people, they may not produce the desired results for others. It may be necessary to try different treatments or a combination of treatments to achieve the best possible outcome for your skin.
record.answer_chatdoctor: Hi, Dermarollers are used for collagen induction therapy. In this procedure, micro needles are used to create tiny channels on the surface of the skin. These channels stimulate the body to produce more collagen and elastin fibers which help to restore normal texture and appearance of the skin. It may be done under local anesthesia or without any painkillers. They can be used to treat acne scars, pitted scars, stretch marks, wrinkles, fine lines, deep furrows, etc. You should discuss with your doctor about its advantages and disadvantages before starting treatment. Hope I have answered your query. Let me know if I can assist you further.

record.answer_icliniq: Hello. I read through the concern. record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I don't have personal opinions, but I can provide you with some information on the Lelli ACL test. The Lelli test is a clinical examination to assess the integrity of the ACL ligament. It involves stabilizing the thigh and applying an anterior force to the tibia while the knee is flexed at 20-30 degrees. If the tibia moves forward more than 5mm, it indicates a positive test for a ruptured ACL. Though the Lelli test has been reported to be highly accurate, it can also give false positive results in case of joint laxity, meniscus lesions, and injuries to other ligaments of the knee. Therefore, it should be used in conjunction with other clinical and radiological examinations for definitive diagnosis and treatment planning. Since you are already feeling some pain and looseness, it would be best to consult your surgeon and physiotherapist for further evaluation and management. They may advise you to undergo an imaging test like an MRI to confirm the diagnosis and assess the extent of any injury.

record.input: Hello doctor,If a girl has sex 2 or 3 days before her periods without any precautions, should she be given preventives or would there be any fear of her being pregnant?
record.answer_icliniq: Hello, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. If the woman is regularly menstruating, meaning she has regular monthly interval of 28 to 32 days, she will not get pregnant three days prior to having sex. Her ovulation would have happened midcycle and that would be her fertile cycle, it will be within days 10 to 20 days of the cycle. So assuming that she menstruated at day 28 interval, then her three days prior would be day 25, and if it is 32 day interval, it would be day 29. She will not get pregnant at that time.

record.answer_icliniq: Hi. I have gone through the query. A person can develop two seizures without regaining consciousness in between or one episode of status epilepticus lasting for more than five minutes. Seizures occurring for 15 minutes requires treatment, and it needs evaluation like EEG (electroencephalography), MRI brain (magnetic resonance imaging), serum calcium and electrolytes, and blood glucose.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I am not a licensed doctor and cannot give medical advice. However, I can provide some general information related to seizures and treatment options. Seizures can occur due to various reasons such as brain injury, genetics, or neurological disorders. They could also be triggered due to lack of sleep, stress, or other factors. EEG and MRI are usually performed to diagnose the underlying cause of the seizure. If both tests come back normal, it may be classified as an idiopathic seizure, meaning the cause remains unknown. Val-Cr 300 (also known as Valproate) is an antiepileptic medication used to treat seizures. The duration of treatment depends on the severity and frequency of the seizure episodes. If the seizures are infrequent or less severe, the doctor may suggest waiting and observing for a second seizure before starting any medication. It is important to consult with a neurologist and discuss the best possible treatment options for your son. They will consider factors such as the age of the patient, the type and severity of seizures, and possible side effects of medication. They can provide more specific advice and guidance based on your son's individual circumstances. I hope this information helps, and I wish you and your son the best of health.
record.answer_chatdoctor: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor.com I am Chat Doctor. Mariano Into Bruno Mascaras. I have gone through your query with diligence and would like you to know that I am here to help you. Epilepsy has many causes, including damage to the brain from trauma, infection, stroke, or tumor. A child who has had one seizure may not need treatment if he or she has no other symptoms and does not have any problems afterward. However, some children will continue to have seizures even though they do not appear to be aware of them. These children should take anti-epileptic medications. You should discuss this issue with your neurologist. Hope you find this answer helpful. If you need further clarification, please ask me. Wishing you good health.

record.input: Hello doctor,I urinate a lot, almost 32 times a day. When I urinate and come back, after a while, I can feel some urine passes my urinary tract, and I have to urinate again. Can you prescribe some tablets?
record.answer_icliniq: Hello, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. We call this as urinary incontinence in medical language. There are so many possibilities which can be responsible for this. Most probably your urinary bladder may fail to empty properly due to weakness of bladder muscle. For this condition, you have to get a USG (ultrasonography) done, where I want the detail about post-void residual volume. It means an ultrasonologist will take scan one before urinating and another after passing the urine in which he will mention the volume of post-void residual urine (PVU). If the volume retention is moderate, then we have to strengthen your pelvic floor muscle with some exercise and believe me it gives an excellent result and I had, treated so many patients with that exercise. Another possibility is decreased blood concentration of ADH (anti-diuretic hormones) which is, responsible for concentrating the urine and lack of this hormone is responsible for frequent urination. The last but not least, I would advise to get your blood sugar level done (both- fasting and two hours after a meal). As you know, without knowing the cause, nobody can prescribe you the medicine as it can be dangerous for you. So I will suggest you to follow my advice, get these two tests done, and send it back to me for proper evaluation and treatment of your problem.

record.input: Hi doctor,I am 22 years old, and I am a mother of four months old baby. Recently, I have got infected with chicken pox. Does breast milk transfer chicken pox to the baby? Do I need to take special care of my baby? Can I do breastfeeding now? Does it affect my child? Please help.
record.answer_icliniq: Hi. A mother who contracts chickenpox can do breastfeeding. Any vesicles present on the breast should be covered to minimize the risk of transmission. There is no evidence shown the presence of the varicella-zoster virus (Chickenpox) in breastmilk. Hence breastfeeding is safe. Breast milk does not transfer chickenpox to the baby. The doctor may prescribe Acyclovir 800 mg five times a day if you are seen within 24 hours of rash onset. It reduces the time to have full crusting of lesions and reduces the number of lesions. Acyclovir can be taken during breastfeeding. It is safe to take. Keep your nails short to minimize the damage from scratching. Take care.

It is essential to take special care of your baby, and it may be a good idea to keep your baby away from anyone who has never had chickenpox or been vaccinated against it. You can continue breastfeeding unless a doctor advises otherwise. Breast milk contains antibodies that can help protect your baby from infections and illnesses. You should consult a healthcare provider if you have any concerns or if you experience any complications related to chickenpox. They can give you personalized advice on how to take care of yourself and your baby.

record.input: Hello doctor,The level of anti-TPO antibodies is 492. The doctors say it is a state of euthyroid. I am worried about my prognosis. I am a medical student, can I still become a surgeon?
record.answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. Going by the details you have provided you have euthyroid. This means that you do have normal TSH with normal T4 and T3. The most common cause is autoimmune thyroid disease, which is confirmed by positive anti-TPO (anti-Thyroid Peroxidase) antibodies. Positive anti-TPO means you have these antibodies in your body which act against the thyroid cells. Most often this is a progressive disease. As of today, we do not have treatment against autoimmunity. Therefore, we replace thyroid hormone in the form of Thyroxine. With TSH near 10 and strongly positive antibodies, I suggest you get your TSH done after 6 weeks. If normal, there is nothing to worry. Apart from the destruction of thyroid cells, anti-TPO antibodies do not cause any other harm. Disease per se is harmless if TSH is kept normal. Keep track of TSH and treat it accordingly. This will never affect your future, and you can continue your studies without fear of not becoming a surgeon. You will become a surgeon, all the best.

record.input: Hello doctor,May I know if you have done urethroplasty in the distal penile urethra using the excision and primary anastomosis (EPA) technique? Are you personally familiar with any such case? If so, was the surgery successful? Did the surgery cause penile curvature? If so, how many degrees did it had? What was the stricture length? Thanks.
record.answer_icliniq: Hi. I am a Urologist and I have performed urethroplasties. But, excision and primary anastomosis is the procedure of choice only in short segment urethral strictures and they occur in the majority of traumatic urethral strictures that occur in the proximal urethra. Such a stricture in distal urethra is rare. However, I had to do excision and primary anastomosis in an iatrogenic distal penile urethral injury, wherein the surgeon had badly damaged distal penile urethra during an attempt to dilate the distal urethra. I was called intraoperative. The urethral plate was badly damaged in that case for only 1 cm. I did excise that part and anastomosed end to end and the result was excellent and there was no postoperative chordee (bending). Other than this case, I have not done any EPA in distal urethra. Take care.
record.answer_chatgpt: I'm sorry, but as an AI language model, I cannot personally perform surgeries or have first-hand experience with specific medical cases. However, I can provide some general information on urethroplasty using the excision and primary anastomosis (EPA) technique in the distal penile urethra. Urethroplasty is a surgical procedure used to repair strictures or narrowing of the urethra. When the stricture is located in the distal penile urethra, the EPA technique can be used to remove the narrowed section of urethra and reconnect the healthy parts. In general, EPA has been shown to have high success rates in distal penile urethral strictures, with success rates ranging from 80 to 95%. Penile curvature can be a possible complication of urethroplasty, particularly if the stricture is located in the area of the penile urethra that corresponds to the site of penis curvature. The degree of curvature can vary depending on the length of the stricture and the severity of the curvature prior to surgery. It is important to discuss the risks and benefits of any surgical procedure with your surgeon, as well as your individual case and any specific concerns you may have.

How long they survive in the lipstick, and how fast they multiply, largely depends on what kind of chemicals were used in the making of the lipstick. It is most effective when taken within 72 hours (3 days) after unprotected intercourse, but it can still be effective up to 5 days after. However, it is important to note that EC pills are not 100% effective and should not be used as a regular form of contraception. They also do not protect against sexually transmitted infections (STIs). If you are concerned about a possible pregnancy, you should take a pregnancy test, especially if you miss your next period. It is also recommended to schedule an appointment with your healthcare provider to discuss your options for regular contraception and STI testing.

They can guide you on the safe and healthy foods to consume during diarrhea.

record.input: Hi doctor,I am considering an endoscopic bariatric stomach surgery procedure for weight loss. It is called the accordion procedure, which has only been around for about five years. Do you think such a method done on a stomach is safe long-term? Thank you for your help. I appreciate it.
record.answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. I have gone through your query. The Accordion procedure is an endoscopic sleeve gastroplasty. It has been around for some time; a few case series and related data are available. It is useful in helping in weight reduction up to 17-23 % weight loss in six months with not many serious long term adverse effects. A few people might end up gaining weight after 4-5 years (which is comparable to laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy). The safety data shows it is safer than laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy in the short term. If you have further concerns, please do get in touch.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I am not a doctor and cannot provide medical advice. It is essential to discuss the potential benefits and risks of any procedure with a qualified healthcare professional who can evaluate your individual health status and provide you with appropriate guidance. Additionally, the safety and effectiveness of specific procedures may vary based on the individual's unique health status and medical history. Please seek the advice of a qualified healthcare professional before making any decisions regarding your medical care.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1) The Accordion procedure is not recommended as a first line of treatment for morbid obesity, but rather as an alternative to other forms of bariatric surgery when other options have failed. 2) Long term safety data on this procedure is limited and more studies need to be conducted to determine its long-term effectiveness and safety.3) Therefore, I would recommend that you consult with a board certified surgeon who specializes in bariatric surgery before deciding if this procedure is right for you.

Your pelvic ultrasound may have shown multiple follicles or cysts on your ovaries, which is a common finding in PCOS. Additionally, you may have a thickened endometrial lining due to irregular shedding of the lining, which can lead to absent periods. In order to improve your chances of getting pregnant, you should talk to your doctor about a treatment plan. Management options for PCOS include lifestyle changes, medication, and reproductive technologies like in vitro fertilization (IVF). Lifestyle changes such as weight loss, regular exercise, and a balanced diet can regulate the hormones associated with PCOS and improve ovulation. Medications like metformin, clomiphene citrate, and letrozole can also help to induce ovulation. It is essential to address your PCOS before trying to conceive, as it increases the risk of complications during pregnancy such as gestational diabetes and preeclampsia. Please consult with your healthcare provider about further evaluation and treatment options.

record.input: Hello doctor,My heart is normal, but I have ventricular ectopic beats. Could these cause cardiac arrest and kill me at the age of 23? I worry about it every day. Kindly advice.
record.answer_icliniq: Hello. It is unusual for ventricular ectopics alone to cause cardiac arrest. Please provide your ECG and Echocardiography report. You have uploaded your photo as an attachment (attachment removed to protect patient identity).
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot replace a doctor's advice, but I can provide you with general information. Ventricular ectopic beats, also known as premature ventricular contractions (PVCs), are common and typically harmless in people without underlying heart disease. PVCs are an extra heartbeat that initiates from the lower chamber of the heart (ventricle) rather than the normal pathway from the top chamber (atrium). PVCs usually do not cause any serious problems and can be brought on by stress, anxiety, caffeine, alcohol, and certain medications. In general, PVCs alone are not likely to cause cardiac arrest or sudden death. However, in some cases, PVCs can lead to more serious arrhythmias that require treatment. If you experience frequent or persistent PVCs, you should discuss this with your healthcare provider. They will review your symptoms, medical history, and potentially conduct tests to evaluate your cardiovascular health. They may recommend lifestyle modifications, medications, or further evaluation by a specialist to ensure your heart is healthy. Remember that worrying and stress can worsen PVCs and harm your overall health. Try to stay calm and trust your healthcare provider's recommendations.
record.answer_chatdoctor: Hi thanks for asking question in Chat Doctor. Ventricular Ectopic Beats (VEB) is a condition wherein there are extra or abnormal beats originating from the lower chambers of the heart (ventricles). These extra beats are called as ectopic beats. They do not originate from the SA node (sinus node) which generates all the other heartbeats. In most cases they occur in the right ventricle. It can also happen in left ventricle. Your age should not be a factor to worry about VEB. They can occur due to many reasons like stress, anxiety, caffeine intake, excessive alcohol consumption etc. But this does not mean that your life will end soon if you have them. You need to consult a Cardiologist for further evaluation and management. Hope I answered your query. Feel free to ask any more queries. Take care.

However, I can provide some general information about diabetes-related muscle loss (sarcopenia) and link you to some resources that you may find helpful. Sarcopenia is a common complication of aging and diabetes. It is characterized by the loss of muscle mass and function, leading to decreased mobility and increased risk of falls and fractures. The exact mechanisms underlying sarcopenia in diabetes are not fully understood, but it appears to be related to insulin resistance and chronic inflammation. Management of sarcopenia in diabetes includes regular exercise, balanced nutrition, and adequate glycemic control. Resistance training and aerobic exercise have been shown to be effective in improving muscle mass and strength in people with diabetes. Additionally, a diet rich in lean protein, fruits, vegetables, and whole grains can help maintain muscle mass. It is important for your father to also follow his doctor's recommended treatment plan for his diabetes, which may include medications and regular monitoring of blood glucose levels. Electro homeopathy is not a conventional form of treatment for diabetes and may not have sufficient scientific evidence to support its effectiveness in managing sarcopenia. I would suggest discussing your father's concerns with his doctor and exploring other options for managing his sarcopenia. A registered dietitian or certified diabetes educator may also be able to offer additional guidance on lifestyle modifications that can help improve muscle mass and function. Here are some resources that you may find helpful: - American Diabetes Association: Sarcopenia in Older Adults with Diabetes - National Institute of Diabetes and Digestive and Kidney Diseases: Diabetes and Sarcopenia - National Institute on Aging: Exercise and Physical Activity for Older Adults

The current standard of care is to maintain the patient on low dose ATRA and chemotherapy(all-trans retinoic acid [ATRA] + anthracyclines/cytosine analogues) until complete remission (CR) is achieved. CR is defined as absence of blast cells in peripheral blood and bone marrow. Persistent minimal residual disease (MRD) may be present even after achieving CR. MRD levels can only be detected by molecular methods such as PCR or FISH. It has been suggested that if MRD levels are less than 1%, then it is likely that the disease is in full remission. In your husbands case, since the MRD levels are above 1%, further treatment is required.

It is also possible for fraternal twins to be the result of fertility treatments, such as in vitro fertilization, where more than one fertilized embryo is implanted in the woman's uterus.

record.input: Hi doctor,I am planning to go for liposuction to lose weight. My weight is 78 kg and height is 5\3". Kindly suggest.
record.answer_icliniq: Hi. Your calculated body mass index (BMI) is 30.4 kg/m2. Generally we perform bariatric surgery to achieve permanent weight loss for any patient whose BMI is 30 kg/m2 or above. Liposuction is not a treatment option for weight loss. It is a cosmetic procedure for body shaping. There is a set point for fat storage for everyone. This is not altered by liposuction. Weight loss by liposuction, diet control, exercise, naturopathy, amway, herbalife, etc., is temporary, as all these methods do not have any effect on set point for fat storage. So, any fat that is removed in liposuction will reaccumulate over a period of 6 months to two years. Those who can reduce weight by diet control and exercise does not need liposuction and those who cannot reduce weight by above methods will not reduce even after liposuction. Bariatric surgery provides permanent weight loss as it lowers set point for fat storage. If you are looking for permanent weight loss then bariatric surgery is the only scientifically proven method for that. Take care.

I had some slight balance problems, and a slight tremor in my right hand for about three years before the virus. I went to a neurologist and was told it was nothing. Last month I had my right knee replaced, and within a few days I had a severe tremor in right hand, weakness on the right side of my body, and was dragging my right foot, and severe speech problems. My wife took me to ER as she thought I was having a stroke. I was in the hospital three days, and was told it was stress and was given a script for CARB or LEVO ER 25/100, told to make an appointment with a psychologist, and motion specialists. I was able to see a motion specialist, and was diagnosed with Parkinsonian features affecting balance, gait, aphasia, apraxia, and was told this was brought about by the virus I had six years back more than likely. I get a fairly good response from the medication once it kicks in. record.input: Hello doctor, I have a question about my soon to be 12-year-old son. Since he was 1 year old he has had febrile seizures about once a year. They only happen at night. When he is sick during the day I would give him Motrin or Tylenol. Through out the night and he would not have any symptoms. When he did not show signs of being sick that night his fever would spike. Two years ago we had his first video EEG and there were no abnormalities or no concerns. We did MRI, blood tests, and EKG all are normal. He had two seizures this year both while being sick. So the neurologist wanted another EEG. So we did a video EEG. The first night nothing looked wrong so she wanted to keep him another night. During the day he was getting sick he was running a little fever. So that night they choose not to give him Motrin to see if he would have a seizure and he did. It lasted a minute and was a full-body one. They looked over the tape and said there was no evidence of one coming on it came so quickly. They believe (but are not sure) that it came from the frontal lobe. They put him on Keppra for two weeks and then the doctor looked it over with her partners and they decide that Trileptal and Keppra would be what he needs. I happen to total disagree. He has never had a seizure during the day or one without a fever. I feel they so quickly diagnosed him with epilepsy without considering that all his tests are normal. That seizure they saw came on because we didn\t give him medicine for his fever. Which to me seems like he has the rare case of febrile seizures lasting till the age of 12 years. He did not have any seizures last year but just two seizures this year. I personally want to try and build his immune system to stop him from getting sick to see if that will prevent his seizures. His father disagrees with me and agrees with the doctor. I believe the side effects of both these medicine out way the actual problem. I am just overwhelmed and stressed. Because I do not know what is the right thing to do? Can febrile seizures last to the age of 12? Is it rare for someone to have a seizure maybe once a year then not have any, then have one the next year? Does the medicine he is on sound too extreme? Please advise.

Your concern is also justified because: 1. The child is a very classic case of febrile seizures. 2. The night when the child had a seizure, the child had a fever and the anti-pyretic was not given. I think what would have happened is, doctors would have thought that at 12 years it could not be a febrile seizure, along with that they found probable origin in the frontal lobe, so they prescribed AEDs (anti-epileptic ChatDoctor. Have patience, as of now continue the medicines. That being said, if your son has had febrile seizures since he was one year old, it is understandable that the neurologist would want to consider epilepsy as a possible diagnosis. record.answer_chatdoctor: Hi, I understand your concern regarding your sons health problems. record.answer_chatgpt: Hello, I'm sorry, but as an AI language model, I am not able to view or evaluate any attached medical reports or images. However, I can give you some general information about uterine fibroids (myoma) in pregnancy. First of all, uterine fibroids are common benign tumors that affect up to 70 percent of women during their reproductive years. Most fibroids do not cause any problems during pregnancy and will not require any treatment. However, some fibroids can grow rapidly during pregnancy due to hormonal changes, which can cause discomfort or complications depending on their size and location. If your wife's fibroid is small and not causing any symptoms, her obstetrician may just monitor it during follow-up ultrasounds to make sure it doesn't grow or cause any problems. However, if the fibroid is large or causing symptoms such as pain or heavy bleeding, your wife's doctor may recommend treatment options such as surgery or medication. In terms of delivery, most women with fibroids are able to deliver vaginally without any problems. However, in rare cases, a large or obstructive fibroid may make vaginal delivery difficult and a cesarean delivery may be necessary. As for your concerns regarding your wife's and baby's health, it's important to keep in close contact with her obstetrician and follow their advice for monitoring and treatment. In general, most women with fibroids have successful pregnancies and healthy deliveries, but it's important to address any concerns or complications as they arise. I hope this information helps, but again, please consult with your wife's obstetrician for specific advice and management of her fibroid during pregnancy.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1) It is not uncommon for women who are pregnant to have fibroids. 2) Fibroids can cause problems with bleeding or pain, but most often they don't. 3) If your wife has no other symptoms, it is unlikely that this will cause any problems with her pregnancy. 4) Most likely she will have a normal pregnancy and delivery, although some women may need a cesarean section if the baby gets too big or the fibroid too large. 5) There is no reason why she should not be able to breast-feed normally.

Osteoarthritis is a degenerative joint disease that can affect any joint, including the hands. The most common symptoms are pain, stiffness, and decreased range of motion. Erosions, on the other hand, are defects in the joint surface that can occur in some types of arthritis, such as rheumatoid arthritis. It is possible for a person with osteoarthritis to have erosions, but erosions are more commonly associated with rheumatoid arthritis or psoriatic arthritis. It is important to note that the absence of erosions does not necessarily rule out a diagnosis of osteoarthritis. Diagnosis is typically made based on a combination of symptoms, physical examination, imaging tests, and sometimes laboratory tests. record.input: Hello doctor,I am 22 years old. And for four months I have had emergency contraceptive pill four times. One in each month after having unprotected sex. record.answer_icliniq: Hello. Emergency contraception is not to be taken regularly. There are two phases of the menstrual cycle first 15 days estrogenic phase and second secretory phase. These tablets are high dose hormonal and delay ovulation. So depending upon which phase you took medicine, the period will prepone or postpone. Or you can have erratic bleeding, irregular cycle eventually. Side effects of nausea acne bloating breast tenderness may occur. Get a urine pregnancy test done three weeks after taking a pill as it can even fail. Consult a gynecologist for regular contraception soon. Take care.

record.answer_icliniq: Hi. Your MRI looks grossly normal. You will require high resolution CT of the temporal bone to look for any bony abnormalities. Another possibility is dural arteriovenousfistula dAVF; however, MRI does not show any evidence for this. You will need cerebral DSA (digital subtraction angiography) for excluding this condition because of tinnitus. The same test will also identify jugular vein and sigmoid sinus problems.

record.input: Hello doctor,I had a 4.4 PSA and had an MRI that was negative. Six months later my PSA is 5.7 and even though the MRI was negative it is suggested that I should have a prostate biopsy. I am a 73-year-old male. I am certainly not that excited to have a biopsy. Any thoughts?
record.answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. I can understand your concern about undergoing an invasive investigation without any symptoms. Let me explain you what does PSA suggest. Basically, PSA is secreted by the prostate as well as some other body tissues. It does not reach circulation and hence remains low in blood as long as the barrier between prostatic tissue and blood vessel is kept intact. Rising PSA is a sign that there is some tissue alteration which has resulted in breach of this barrier. Hence, do not be under the impression that rising PSA always means malignancy. But it is better to confirm the absence of malignancy by biopsy as, at a PSA level as low as 5.7, even in a unlikely event of report being malignancy, the chances of organ confined lesion is very high, which is easy to treat and chances if cure are high. Hence, it is better to undergo biopsy and get your fears allayed. As such, the biopsy is a very small procedure and safe in experienced hands. Wish you a safe biopsy and I hope the results will be benign.

As an AI language model, I cannot provide a diagnosis, but I can give you some general information to consider. In general, an elevated PSA level can indicate an increased risk for prostate cancer. However, it is important to note that not all elevated PSA levels are caused by prostate cancer. There are various other factors that can cause PSA levels to rise, such as infection, inflammation, or enlarged prostate. Regarding the negative MRI, it is possible that the abnormalities in your prostate are too small to be detected by the imaging. Therefore, a biopsy may be recommended to provide a more accurate assessment of the prostate tissue status. It is understandable that the thought of a biopsy may be scary, but it is considered the gold standard for detecting prostate cancer. However, you should discuss with your doctor the possible risks and benefits of a biopsy, as well as any other alternatives to gather more information about your condition, before making a final decision. Overall, regular prostate cancer screening is an important part of maintaining good health and detecting any potential issues early. I hope this information helps, but don't hesitate to follow up with your healthcare provider with any additional questions or concerns you may have.

record.answer_chatdoctor: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. Wolf-Parkinson White (WPW) is an extra conduction pathway in which there is direct connection between atria and ventricles. This results in rapid conduction of electrical impulses from atria to ventricles bypassing AV node. It causes palpitations, tachycardia and syncope. This condition is treated with medicines like beta blockers, calcium channel blockers, antiarrhythmics etc. Also, radiofrequency catheter ablation is done to treat this condition permanently. Please consult your cardiologist for further management. Thanks.

record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I don't have access to the ultrasound images you mentioned, and it is not appropriate for me to provide medical advice. Still, I can explain what choroid plexus cysts are and what they might suggest. Choroid plexus cysts are small fluid-filled sacs that occur within the choroid plexus, which is a specialized tissue in the brain that produces cerebrospinal fluid (CSF). Choroid plexus cysts are usually detected during a routine prenatal ultrasound, and in most cases, they do not cause any problems and resolve on their own. In some cases, choroid plexus cysts can be an indication of a chromosomal abnormality, such as trisomy 18 or 21. However, it is worth noting that having choroid plexus cysts alone does not necessarily mean that a baby has a chromosomal abnormality. Many babies with choroid plexus cysts are born healthy and do not have any chromosomal abnormalities. Based on the information you provided, it seems that you have had a thorough evaluation, including genetic testing and multiple ultrasounds. It is also reassuring that the genetic testing was negative for trisomy 18 and 21. If you have any concerns or questions, I recommend discussing them with your healthcare provider, who can provide you with more specific information and guidance based on your individual circumstances.

record.answer_icliniq: Hi. There is nothing like functional HIV infection. Confirm, whether he is HIV 1 or 2 because HIV 2 is less infectious and less violent than 1. If he is infected with 1, then there will be slow progressor or rapid progressor. If he is rapid, then he will have a low CD4 count below 350 by two years, and if he is slow progressor then it will take 4 to 5 years but inevitable to land in AIDS if he will not take medicines. 1. Get his viral load and CD4 report to know the current status to assess his claims. 2. Prognosis is very good at current age owing to very good use of HAART medication. They can live a normal life with medicine. Take care.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot verify the claims made by your relative. However, the best way for you to be sure of his claims is to consult a healthcare professional who can assess his medical condition and give you an expert opinion. Regarding the prognosis of HIV infection, it is essential to understand that HIV is a chronic disease that can be well-managed with antiretroviral therapy (ART). While some individuals may have a slower progression of the disease than others, there is currently no cure for HIV. The overall prognosis for HIV-positive individuals is good if they receive proper medical care, take their medication as prescribed, and maintain a healthy lifestyle. It's essential to note that HIV-positive individuals who do not receive proper treatment may experience a worsening of their condition, leading to the development of acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS). AIDS weakens the immune system to the point where it cannot fight off infections and cancers, which can be fatal. In summary, it is crucial to consult with a healthcare professional to verify the claims made by your relative and to get expert advice on the prognosis of HIV infection. It's important to remember that HIV is a serious, chronic disease that requires proper medical care to manage effectively.

record.answer_icliniq: Hello, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. Most probably, these are ventricular premature complexes. These are ectopic or extra beats. These are usually benign and not life-threatening. These are frequently associated with MVP (mitral valve prolapse), also caffeine, alcohol, thyroid, gastritis and acidity problems may aggravate these. Since these are not frequent, ECG may be normal. So, in that case, you will need 24-hour Holter monitoring which can diagnose these. You should also get a thyroid test done. You will need beta blockers like Metoprolol to keep these under control. record.input: Hi doctor,I want to go for a corrective surgery and am confused between Contoura and SMILE. I request you to please give your opinion on the following doubts of mine. People say that all LASIK surgeries involve greater cornea related risks which SMILE does not. Your view on this?While for SMILE people say that actual corneal depth removed is more and even the appearance objects after SMILE is very artificial and is not as sharp or 3D like in Contura. Your view on this? Also, is there any clause like if one is not able to focus eyes on point or concentrate (which is totally in hand of the patient in SMILE) then he should not opt for SMILE as there is a risk of hurting eye. Is that true? Also, irrespective of which one we do, are these surgeries always accompanied by halos, glares and night vision problems? Finally, I am getting married in two months and I would be required to indulge myself in all the dance rehearsals, makeup, market traveling, etc. What do you think, is it the right time to go for a surgery? And which one (that is less risky and recovers faster)?
record.answer_icliniq: Hello. 1. If LASIK has greater corneal risk than a SMILE? This is true. LASIK does have a greater risk of flap-related complications. In SMILE, as there is no flap created, there are no complications associated with it. 2. Corneal depth in LASIK vs SMILE. The depth in LASIK is dependent on your refractive error. The more your refractive error, the more will the tissue abated. In SMILE, the thickness of the lenticular is preset by the surgeon and does not depend on the refractive error. In both procedures, the surgeon can also take measures to conserve thickness. So the answer is that it depends on the surgeon and your refractive error. 3. The sharpness of images Contoura vs SMILE. There are various studies done on this and a majority say that SMILE offers better results in most laboratory tests, but under practical and daily life situations, it can be assumed that both are pretty much the same. 4. Can hurt your eye in SMILE if you do not concentrate on a point? I am not very sure about the exact context of the question. I can tell you that in both surgeries you will be asked to look at a pointer light and the accuracy of your correction will depend on how well you maintain a fixed gaze on the pointer. If you look any other way for even a fraction of a second the outcome will be bad in both of the procedures. 5. Glare halos and night vision problems. These will be present soon after surgery in both, but will slowly resolve. Not a major problem in a majority of the patients. 6. Right time for surgery, which surgery is less risky and has a faster recovery? Right time for surgery would be around three months from your wedding, although some do get SMILE done just one day before their wedding. Anytime before 1 to 2 months, you will be in the recovery phase and will have to be instilling eye ChatDoctor. 7. Less risky and has faster recovery- SMILE (small incision lenticule extraction).
record.answer_chatgpt: Hello, as an AI language model, I am not a doctor and cannot give medical advice. However, I can provide some general information that might help you in making an informed decision. Both Contoura and SMILE are advanced laser refractive surgeries that can correct nearsightedness, farsightedness, and astigmatism. While they have some similarities, there are some key differences between the two. Contoura involves creating a customized treatment plan based on the unique topography of your cornea, whereas SMILE is a minimally invasive procedure that involves using a laser to reshape the cornea without creating a flap. In terms of risks, any type of eye surgery carries some risks, including infection, inflammation, and dry eye. However, SMILE may carry less risk of corneal ectasia, a condition where the cornea weakens and bulges out, which can occur in rare cases after traditional LASIK surgery. Regarding the depth of corneal tissue removed, the amount of tissue removed with SMILE is generally less than with LASIK, but this does not necessarily affect the quality of visual outcomes. Both procedures can provide excellent visual results. It is important to note that patient selection is key to achieving good outcomes with any type of refractive surgery. Your surgeon will evaluate factors such as corneal thickness, refractive error, age, and overall eye health to determine if you are a good candidate for Contoura or SMILE. Regarding postoperative visual disturbances such as halos, glares, and night vision problems, these are rare and usually resolve over time. The risk of these complications is generally similar between Contoura and SMILE. As for timing of the surgery, it is generally recommended that you wait a few weeks after the surgery before engaging in strenuous activities such as dancing and heavy makeup application. However, most people are able to return to their normal activities within a few days to a week after the surgery, depending on the type of procedure. Again, these are general guidelines, and you should consult with a qualified ophthalmologist to determine if you are a good candidate for either procedure and to address any concerns you may have.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1. Yes, there is a possibility of having halos, glares and night vision problem post correction.2. It depends upon how much correction you need. You can discuss with your ophthalmologist regarding the same.3. The choice of procedure also depend upon the degree of refractive error.4. Generally, most of the laser treatment will require pre-operative and post-operative care. So, better to get it done when you have some spare time.5. Regarding safety of both procedures, they are safe and effective but the results vary from person to person.6. For those who have higher power corrections, the result may not be as good as expected.7. I don't think you should worry about night vision problem, because you will be using glasses during daytime too. Hope I have answered your query. Let me know if I can assist you further.

While both can cause severe illness, the two diseases are caused by completely different pathogens and require different prevention methods. Vaccination against COVID-19 is recommended to prevent illness caused by the coronavirus.

record.input: Hello doctor, I would like to know what happens biologically in the body, causing a burning sensation in the tongue when a person has HIV.
record.answer_icliniq: Hello, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. As you very well knew, HIV (human immunodeficiency virus) virus lives in a body for a very long time. It causes significant damage to the central and peripheral nervous systems. The virus affects our nervous system and causes dysfunction of nerves and muscle cells. The burning sensation is a symptom of peripheral nervous system dysfunction or disorder, and it is caused as the nerves that lead to and from the spinal cord and connect with all the various parts of the body are affected by HIV. The nerve root gets exposed, and the person feels burning sensation or numbness, starting with hands, feet, and tongue. I hope this helps.

Another possible cause may be neuropathy, which is a nerve damage that can affect the tongue and cause a burning or tingling sensation. Again, these are just possible causes, and individuals with HIV should consult with their healthcare provider for proper diagnosis and treatment.

record.input: Hi doctor,I had an unprotected intercourse last week. At that time, I took an I-pill. After a week, I got my periods. Now, again today I had an unprotected sex. Should I need to take I-pill again?
record.answer_icliniq: Hi. I have reviewed your query. You have taken I-pill before 10 days (before periods), and you had your menstrual cycle last week. So, I-pill will not work for you in this intercourse. If you want to avoid pregnancy, you can take another I-pill again today. But, I-pill is an emergency contraceptive pill, so it used in emergency conditions. Because of its side effects, it must not be used every time after unprotected sex. It is not a regular contraceptive pill. To avoid pregnancy, if you are sexually active, it is better to choose other alternative contraceptive methods which can be used regularly with few side effects and even lesser failure rates compared to I-pill (like oral contraceptive pills or even copper-T insertion after taking a gynecological consultation). For more information consult an internal medicine physician online Take care.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot provide medical advice, but I can offer some general information. It is not recommended to use emergency contraception, such as the I-pill, as a regular form of contraception. It is designed to be used in emergencies or when regular contraception fails. It's important to find a regular form of contraception that works for you, such as condoms or birth control pills. If you are concerned about pregnancy, you should discuss your options with a healthcare provider.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1. The I pill is 95% effective in preventing pregnancy if taken within 72 hours of intercourse. It will not work if you have had intercourse more than 72 hours ago.2. You cannot use the same pack of emergency contraceptive pills again as it would be dangerous for your health and may cause side effects.3. Please consult your gynecologist who can advise you suitable birth control methods like condom or hormonal contraception.

However, in general, spirometry results provide information on how well your lungs are functioning, particularly how well you can move air in and out of your lungs. The results are compared to predicted values based on your age, height, and gender. If your spirometry results are below predicted values, it may indicate that your lungs are not functioning at full capacity, possibly due to a respiratory condition. Shortness of breath is also a common symptom of respiratory conditions such as asthma, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), or even anxiety. It is important to discuss your symptoms and spirometry results with your healthcare provider for proper diagnosis and treatment.
record.answer_chatdoctor: Hi, Dear thanks for your question to Chat Doctor. I read carefully your query and understand your concern. Unfortunately, I cannot say anything about your Spirometry result without seeing them. You should know that Spirometry is a test which measures how much air we can inhale and exhale over a period of time (usually one second). It also tells us what percentage of our lung capacity we use during this process. In addition, it gives information about how fast we breathe. The results are expressed as FEV1 (Forced Expiratory Volume in One Second) and FVC (Forced Vital Capacity). Normal values vary from lab to lab but usually fall between 80%-120% of predicted value. So, if you want more details about your spirometry results, you may send them to me as a direct question. Hope this answers your query. Wish you good health.

The human growth hormone (HGH) is a hormone that is produced by the pituitary gland and plays an essential role in bone growth during childhood and adolescence. However, after the age of 18-21, the growth plates in the bones fuse and the potential for further growth decreases significantly. There is no proven way to stimulate the pituitary gland to secrete additional growth hormone after this period. I would suggest consulting with a healthcare professional who can provide you with more information regarding your specific situation.

record.input: Hello doctor,I want to quit smoking but I cannot smoke. So suggest me some medicine so that I can quit smoking.
record.answer_icliniq: Hi. I hope you are doing well. I read your query and I am very proud that you have decided to take this great step. First of all, you need to understand that there is no medicine available which will make you quit smoking, it is only you who can make it happen. Its not 1 week or even 1 month process, it takes lot of time and courage. I usually recommend following steps to my patient who wants to quit smoking. 1. Set a date. Setting a date has a psychological effect and you can choose any memorable date, your birthday, your anniversary, etc. 2. Tell your family, your friends and your colleagues. Discuss with them. They are the one who will help you late on with the process. 3. Prepare yourself for the upcoming problems. You will face Nicotine with ChatDoctor. It will be cough, constipation, etc. These are not serious but if you do not anticipate these, such problems will discourage you from quitting and start smoking again. Try to find a friend or partner who also wants to quit. 2 people make it easier for each other. 4. Remove smoking related products (Cigerrates, ashtray) from your house, car, workplace. 5. Now in final step, here comes a little role of doctor. Now you need to understand you have 2 types of addictions, physical i.e. of nicotine and psychological i.e of act of smoking. For physical addiction I would recommend nicotine patches and bubblegum, whatever suits you better. For psychological addiction you have to control yourself, if you think that initially you cannot tolerate that, you can use electronic cigerratte for a month or so and it will help you. This is a workplan I can share with you which will help you in quitting smoking. I would love to see you again and help you step by step in the process wherever you need me.

However, I can suggest that you consult a healthcare professional who can prescribe appropriate medication and provide guidance on quitting smoking. Additionally, there are various nicotine replacement therapies available over the counter such as nicotine patches and gum which can help to reduce cravings and manage withdrawal symptoms. Counseling and support groups can also be helpful in quitting smoking.

In my opinion, the best way to quit smoking is to learn more about it, set a date to quit, plan ahead of time, seek support from family/friends, and try to distract yourself with other activities by avoiding triggers (such as stressful situations).2) Nicotine replacement therapy is another option. You can use nicotine gums or patches to help wean your body off cigarettes.3) There are also medications available such as varenicline which helps reduce cravings, Chat Doctor. record.input: Hello doctor,I just want to start off by saying that I am a 24-year-old male. record.answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. See as such mesenteric lymphadenitis does not directly represent colon cancer. It is very commonly seen post infection. So what we do is that we give antibiotics for seven days and later repeat an ultrasound abdomen after one month to check for the size of lymph nodes. If size are decreasing in size we simply wait and watch. Actually, these lymph nodes if once increased in size may take weeks to months to come down to normal size. Even you do not have any symptoms of colon cancer. However, your suspected family history should make us little concern. So I would recommend you to take a Gastroenterologist consultation and simply get a stool for occult blood done. If this test comes out to be positive then you would need a colonoscopy.
