record.input: Hi doctor,Do you have any experience in HIV 2? I have done my HIV 2 DNA/RNA PCR test and antibody test which came back both negative after three months. What I am worried is HIV 2 is a slowly replicating virus and worry that HIV 2 DNA/RNA PCR test cannot pick up even after three months. Is it true? Am I negative for HIV 2 if both tests comes negative after three months of exposure? Please advise.
record.answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. The replication of the virus has no relationship in the detection of the virus. If the virus is present in the body, the immune system will detect it, and start defending the body by producing antibodies. This procedure does not need any replication of the virus. It just needs a virus in the body. So, if the fourth-generation test is negative by three months and six months, then you are negative. No need to worry about them.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I do not have personal experiences, but I can provide you with information on HIV 2. HIV 2 is a less common type of the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) compared to HIV-1, but it is still present in some parts of the world. The accuracy of the HIV-2 DNA/RNA PCR test is generally higher than the antibody test for detecting HIV-2 infection. However, the possibility of a false-negative result cannot be completely excluded. According to CDC guidelines, a person is considered to be HIV-negative if both the HIV-2 DNA/RNA PCR test and the antibody test are negative 3 months after the last possible exposure to HIV-2. If you have any further concerns, it is best to consult with your healthcare provider for further evaluation and advice.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1. HIV-2 is not as easily detected by antibody testing as HIV-1. This means that the initial diagnosis may be delayed.2. The viral load can be evaluated using HIV RNA assay.3. You need to repeat the test at least once again to confirm your results.4. A negative result does not mean that you are completely protected against HIV-2 infection.5. Repeated negative testing is necessary to exclude HIV-2 infection.6. In case of repeated negative test results, further evaluation is not required.

However, in general, the best imaging modality for detecting a sports hernia may depend on a number of factors such as the availability of the imaging modality, the experience of the imaging specialist, and the specific needs of the patient. Each of the three imaging modalities you mentioned above (dynamic ultrasound, 3T MRI, and CT scan) has its own advantages and disadvantages. Dynamic ultrasound is a relatively inexpensive and non-invasive imaging technique that can provide real-time images of the soft tissues of the hip and groin area. It may be particularly useful in identifying soft tissue injuries such as muscle tears and may be performed during physical examination. However, it may be less effective in identifying bony abnormalities. A 3T MRI is a more sensitive imaging technique that can provide detailed images of the bones, soft tissues, and internal organs of the body. It may be particularly useful in identifying structural abnormalities such as bone fractures, cartilage injuries, and hernias. However, it may be more expensive and time-consuming than ultrasound, and patients may need to lie still for a long time during the imaging procedure. A CT scan can provide detailed images of the bones and internal organs in the body, and may be particularly useful in identifying bony injuries or abnormalities. However, like MRI, it may be more expensive and expose patients to higher levels of radiation. Ultimately, the best imaging modality to detect a sports hernia may depend on the individual patient's clinical presentation and the expertise of the imaging specialist. It is important to discuss your individual situation with your healthcare provider to determine the most appropriate imaging modality for your specific needs.

The window period varies from person to person and can last up to 3-6 months. Since your test was done at 12 weeks (or 3 months), it is very likely that it is accurate, and HIV infection can be ruled out. However, some guidelines suggest repeating the test after six months to be 100% sure. This is because there may be some rare cases where HIV antibodies take longer to develop. If you are still anxious, you could discuss with a healthcare provider to get more personalized advice. Also, always practice safe sex to protect yourself and your sexual partners from sexually transmitted infections.

record.input: Hi doctor,Is that fine to bring peanut butter (PB) on an airplane? I would like to know the ways that bringing PB on a plane might or might not pose a threat to others? What should you do if you are bringing PB and if you have an allergy and want to keep safe in case someone else brings some on the plane? Thanks.
record.answer_icliniq: Hi. You have asked an interesting question. Peanut allergy is one of the most severe food allergies. It can cause itchiness, urticaria, swelling, eczema, sneezing, asthma, abdominal pain, ChatDoctor. It may even result in anaphylactic death if not controlled at the right time. The routes of exposure of the allergen are mostly the accidental ingestion of peanuts or PB by the concerned person (who is hypersensitive to peanuts). Air-borne particles in a farm or factory or crushing environment may cause respiratory symptoms in allergic individuals. However, the odor of peanut butter or its mere proximity has not produced any allergic symptoms in various studies conducted. Hence answering your questions. Take care.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot advise or give advice but what I can do is to inform you that bringing peanut butter on an airplane may or may not pose a threat to others, particularly those with peanut allergies. Peanut butter is not prohibited by the TSA as long as it follows the 3-1-1 liquids rule and is in a container smaller than 3.4 ounces. However, it is important to note that peanut allergies are a serious concern for some individuals, and exposure to even a small amount of peanut particles can trigger an allergic reaction, ranging from mild to life-threatening. If you are bringing PB and you or someone else on the plane has an allergy, it is important to take precautions to avoid cross-contamination or exposure. Some steps you can take include: - Informing the airline of your allergy or requesting a special accommodation if you have a peanut allergy - Bringing your own food and snacks that are safe for you to eat - Avoiding touching surfaces or items that may have come in contact with peanuts or peanut butter - Carrying necessary medications (e.g., epinephrine auto-injector) in case of an allergic reaction. Furthermore, it is always a good idea to be mindful and considerate of others around you, and to check with your airline or the TSA for any specific policies or guidelines regarding bringing peanut butter or other allergens on the plane.
record.answer_chatdoctor: Peanuts can cause allergic reactions in individuals who are sensitive to them. If you are concerned about having an allergic reaction to peanuts during your flight, then it is best to avoid eating any food products containing peanuts while traveling. You may also wish to consider wearing a medic alert tag indicating that you are allergic to peanuts. As far as other passengers being aware of your condition, it will be up to you to inform them that you are allergic to peanuts.

These tests will determine if you have an HGH deficiency. If you do, your doctor may recommend HGH replacement therapy. At home, he has a very bad temper and tries to fight with everyone. The only one he gets along with is one of his brothers. He has threatened to kill himself because he does not have his laptop anymore, it broke. And he has also stated along the lines that he wants to kill our parents because it is their fault, he is like this. To be fair, our parents are very religious. I do not know how to help him because he does not want help. He says no one cares about him and we all want his life to be bad. What do I do to help?
record.answer_icliniq: Hello, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. It can be difficult to see your family going through challenging circumstances. From your report, it looks like your teenaged brother is struggling to regulate his behavior, blames others and is not able to take responsibility for his actions. As you may know already, there is no quick fix solution. Try to be supportive and available whenever your brother needs you. He is lucky to have an understanding sister like you. He needs to gently changed when he says no one cares about him. Why does he think so? Is he misinterpreting things? You said he gets on well with one of his brothers. Use it positively and involve this brother in getting help for your troubled sibling. Encourage him to enroll in some course, apprenticeship which will give some positive experience for him. Encourage him to join some sports clubs. Discuss with your parents about adopting a supportive, firm, boundaried relationship with your brother and avoid criticism and confrontational interactions. It may help to have a mentor to talk to him regularly and guide him to make the right choices. It can be any responsible adult whom your brother trusts and will talk to. Finally, encourage him to see a counselor or doctor again if problems persist.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot diagnose or provide a treatment plan for your brother. However, I suggest that you encourage him to talk to a professional who specializes in mental health issues, such as a psychiatrist or a psychologist. You can also contact a local mental health facility or a crisis helpline. Additionally, you can reach out to a trusted adult, such as a teacher, counselor, or family member, to discuss your concerns for your brother's safety and well-being. It's essential to take any threats of harming himself or others seriously and seek immediate help.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1. It is good that you want to help your brother.2. You said that he was seen by a psychiatrist and he was prescribed medicines.3. Medicines can help in reducing symptoms.4. But the most important thing is for him to be motivated to change.5. For this, you need to find out what he likes or loves doing so that you can motivate him to work towards that goal.6. Once he has something to look forward to, he will be more willing to change.7. If he is not interested in anything then you may try to involve him in some activities such as sports, music etc. which he might enjoy.8. If you think that he is too angry, irritable, depressed or anxious to manage at home, then you should take him to a hospital where he can stay for some days (may be a week) so that his anger and aggression can be controlled.9

record.input: Hi,I was supposed to get my period on the 19th of this month. Generally, my period lasts 25 days, but it did not arrive this month.After taking a urine test using a pregnancy detection kit, I received a positive result. So, I think I am pregnant. I have not taken a scan or contacted a doctor. I do not want to carry my pregnancy.Is it necessary to have a scan performed before taking abortion pills or can I take the pills directly? If so, can you please make suggestions as to the pills I should take and how to use them? Also, what is the last date for me to terminate my pregnancy using pills? If there are any side effects, please mention them as well.
record.answer_icliniq: Hi and welcome to ChatDoctor. Since a urine pregnancy test result was positive, you are pregnant. However, it is very important to confirm the same by scan and to locate the pregnancy to make sure it is in the uterus and not outside, which is called an ectopic pregnancy. Ectopic pregnancies cannot be aborted by pills and they present a dangerous situation, which requires immediate medical attention. You can terminate an intra-uterine pregnancy with pills up to 63 days after the first day of your last period. Following that, failure rates are high. The earlier the termination, the less risk of complications. Pills for termination cannot be prescribed over the phone or online, as it is illegal and dangerous. I would also advise you to consult an obstetrician and gynaecologist online about receiving counseling on ending your pregnancy
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot provide medical advice. It's important for you to visit a healthcare provider to receive proper evaluation and counseling regarding your pregnancy options. Depending on your location, there may be legal requirements for obtaining an ultrasound or other tests before an abortion can be provided. However, if you cannot afford a visit to a healthcare provider, there are organizations that offer telemedicine or mail-based abortion, such as TelAbortion and Planned Parenthood. It's also important to note that abortion pills should only be used under the supervision of a healthcare provider. Self-administering abortion pills can be both dangerous and ineffective. Abortifacient medications, such as mifepristone and misoprostol, can only be prescribed by a healthcare professional, and it is recommended that they only be taken under medical supervision. The gestation period determines the available abortion methods, so the earlier the abortion, the more options you have. The abortion pill is used up to ten weeks after the first day of the last menstrual period. However, the sooner you have medical care, the more options that will be available to you. There are several side effects of abortion pills, including cramping, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and heavy bleeding. These side effects are usually mild to moderate and should disappear after a few days. Consulting a healthcare provider to discuss your options is strongly recommended.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1) Yes it is possible that you may be pregnant.2) You must confirm your pregnancy by ultrasonography/blood tests.3) It is advisable to consult an obstetrician / gynecologist who will advise you regarding termination of pregnancy with medicines.4) Medicines like Mifepristone followed by Misoprostol (Cytotec) is used for early abortion up to 7 weeks of pregnancy.5) The pill regimen is available in the form of tablets and also in vaginal suppository forms.6) After taking the first medicine, you need to come back after 48 hours to take the second medicine.7) The last day to take the pill is usually 10 weeks from the first day of last menstrual period.8) Side effects include heaviness in the abdomen, cramps, nausea, vom

record.input: Hello doctor,I am a 30-year-old female with a height of 5 feet 3 inches and a weight of 65 pounds. This is my first pregnancy. All test reports are normal until now but my BP is 140/90. Is it very high? I am too much tensed. Please help me.
record.answer_icliniq: Hello. Fourteen weeks pregnancy and blood pressure is 140/90. You need to monitor your blood pressure every six hours with your gynaecologist who will judge whether it is really raised or just a chance of finding. Accordingly, he will prescribe you medicines. You may start with tablet Aspirin 75 mg to maintain your blood pressure and fetal growth parameters. Consult your specialist doctor, discuss with him or her and with their consent take the medicines. You need to have diet changes such as low salt diet, no pickles and spicy food. For more information consult an obstetrician and gynaecologist online
record.answer_chatgpt: Hello! As an AI language model, I cannot provide medical advice or diagnosis. However, I can give you some important information and guidelines. High blood pressure during pregnancy is a condition called gestational hypertension. A BP measurement of 140/90 is considered high for pregnant women. High blood pressure can have serious consequences on pregnancy and delivery, including preeclampsia and premature birth, which can be harmful to both the mother and baby. You must consult your healthcare provider to discuss your blood pressure concerns. Your healthcare provider may suggest lifestyle modifications, regular monitoring, and medications to manage your blood pressure. It is essential to follow your provider's recommendations and treatment plan to ensure both you and your baby's wellbeing. Additionally, try to manage your stress and anxiety as much as possible. Consider practicing relaxation techniques and mindfulness to cope with your worries and promote your mental health. Please seek medical advice promptly and follow your health care professional's advice for the best possible outcome.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 140/90 mmHg is not considered to be high for pregnancy. It would have been considered abnormal if your blood pressure had been above 150/100 mmHg. However, there are certain factors that can contribute to an increase in blood pressure during pregnancy like emotional stress, increased body mass, salt intake etc. So you should try to avoid these factors as far as possible. You may also need to take some medicines like methyldopa or labetalol to control blood pressure.

I do not know either milk is coming or not. Why is it coming suddenly? I feel it may be an indication of HIV. It happened only after the incident. I was thinking in two ways. Could it be due to my liquid which went inside her mouth, if she has an infection? Or maybe due to that, I have sucked her breast I felt I might have drunk her milk. Is this an initial infection of HIV? Kindly look at my attachment. Please advice.
record.answer_icliniq: Hello, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. As per your exposure, you seem to be safe from HIV. The thigh lesions are not due to HIV (attachment removed to protect patient identity). It could be due to other causes. Do you have any symptoms like pain, itching, etc.?
record.answer_chatgpt: Hello, as an AI language model, I'm not a doctor and cannot diagnose or provide medical advice. However, I can tell you that a red pimple on your inner thigh is unlikely to be a sign of HIV infection. HIV is typically transmitted through blood, semen, vaginal fluids, and breast milk. It is possible to contract HIV through oral sex, but it is not common. You should consult a healthcare professional to determine the cause of the pimple and to discuss any concerns you may have about your sexual health. It's important to practice safe sex by using condoms and getting tested regularly for sexually transmitted infections.

The fact that you also had a red spot on your chest indicates that you may have had a similar reaction there also.4. There is no need to worry about HIV transmission.

record.input: Hello doctor,Can the rabies virus survive outside the host animal under sunlight?
record.answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. Rabies virus is not very robust under external environmental conditions. Direct sunlight and lack of humidity are very deleterious to its survival. It might survive for a couple hours or so, but there is no definitive proof. Rather what is important are the precautions that one should take if contracts virus. For example, washing contacts with soap and water are extremely essential. If the directly contacted skin is broken or cut then one may consider vaccination too. Let me know if you have any further queries.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot confirm individual cases or provide medical advice. However, the rabies virus is generally considered to be fragile and is susceptible to environmental conditions such as sunlight, heat, and drying out. It usually does not survive for long outside the host animal, especially under direct sunlight. But it's important to note that if you have been exposed to the virus, seek immediate medical attention regardless of the circumstances in which the virus may have been found.
record.answer_chatdoctor: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor. Rabies is an infectious disease caused by a neurotropic virus of the Rhabdoviridae family. It is transmitted by bite of an infected dog. The virus is destroyed in sunlight and cannot survive outside the host for more than one hour. So there is no chance of transmission from sunlight. I hope this information will be useful to you. Thank you.

record.input: Hello doctor,I am 50 years with PAF. My first A-Fib was five years ago. I converted on my own. It went away for five years and in the year end, I started a keto diet and went into A-Fib twice and converted on my own. Started keto again and went into A-fib twice again and converted on my own. My cardiologist has me on 50 mg Flecainide twice a day. I am also on Prozac 20 mg and Trazadone 400 mg. I am concerned about developing Prolonged QT, and I am not sure if Flecainide is really necessary? I am worried about what Flecainide will do to my heart. I have a healthy heart otherwise. 60 resting heart rate and 95/70 BP.
record.answer_icliniq: Hello, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. I passed carefully through your medical history and would like to explain that you are right when you worry about your current medications. Both Prozac and Flecainide increase QTc interval and may predispose for arrhythmia. I would recommend stop continuing their concomitant use. Probably it would be better to start with ChatDoctor. But if it is not possible for the moment, you should discuss with your attending doctor to keep the most indispensable
record.answer_chatgpt: Hello! As an AI language model, I cannot offer medical advice. However, I can provide some information that might be useful to you. Flecainide is a medication that is commonly prescribed to prevent and treat atrial fibrillation (AF). It works by suppressing irregular heartbeats and restoring normal heart rhythm. However, as with any medication, it can have some side effects. One of the potential side effects of Flecainide is the risk of developing a prolonged QT interval, which can increase the risk of arrhythmias and sudden cardiac death. It is important to monitor your heart rhythm if you are taking Flecainide and report any symptoms or changes in your heart rhythm to your doctor. They may also order regular electrocardiograms (ECGs) to monitor your heart rhythm. Regarding whether Flecainide is necessary, it is best to discuss this with your doctor. They will consider your individual situation and medical history to determine the best treatment plan for you. It is important to follow your doctor's instructions regarding medication use and to report any concerns or side effects to them promptly.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1) You should be evaluated by an electrophysiologist or a cardiac surgeon to see if you need a procedure or medicines like flecainide. If your ECG shows prolonged QT interval then you may need to take flecainide as it helps prevent sudden death from arrhythmia. 2) Keto diet is known to cause irregular heartbeats and hence you were advised to take flecainide.3) There are many newer medications that can help control AF apart from flecainide. You can discuss with your doctor regarding other options.

Secondly, the likelihood of getting gonorrhea and other infections which you mentioned are very unlikely after three months. If you had developed gonorrhea, there would be discharge or bumps over the penis within one month. About oral HPV, if your sexual partner had this infection, then there is a possibility of transmission, but that too is very unlikely. If you had developed, you would have symptoms by now. There would be at least minute warts that would have appeared over the oral cavity or genitalia. So, do not get tensed. Be cool. record.answer_chatdoctor: Hi, Thanks for writing to Chat Doctor. The pus cells count of 3 to 6 is within normal limits. Yes, you can take the test for Chlamydia and Trichomonas, which will help you to diagnose the infection. Your results will come back in few days. You don't need to worry about it. The oral HPV virus is transmitted through kissing, but it does not lead to any serious health problems. It usually goes away without treatment. Hope this helps. Take care

record.input: Hello doctor,A guy penetrates my vagina without protection and I do not know his status. It was for approximately for 10 seconds. What are the chances of contracting HIV and should I get a PEP?
record.answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. You had a low risk for HIV acquisition. Studies have shown that risk of HIV acquisition through receptive vaginal sex (receiving the penis in vagina) to be 0.08 % (one per 1250 exposures). Post-exposure prophylaxis needs to be started as early as possible within 72 hours of the exposure.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I am not a doctor and cannot provide medical advice. However, I can give you some general information about the risks of contracting HIV and the use of PEP. The risk of contracting HIV from a single unprotected sexual encounter is relatively low, but it is not zero. The actual risk depends on various factors, such as the viral load of the person with HIV and the presence of other sexually transmitted infections. Post-exposure prophylaxis (PEP) is a combination of antiretroviral drugs given to people who may have been exposed to HIV through sexual contact, needle-sharing, or other means. PEP can reduce the likelihood of HIV infection, but it is most effective when started as soon as possible after exposure (ideally within 72 hours) and taken consistently. If you are concerned about your risk of HIV infection, it is best to talk to a healthcare provider who can evaluate your individual situation and recommend appropriate testing and treatment options.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 2nd possibility is that it depends on the route of transmission, if he had unprotected sex with another person then you need to be concerned about STD or HIV infection. The risk of being infected with HIV by sexual contact is 5-7%, but there is no reason to worry since you were protected. There is no need for PEP (post exposure prophylaxis) as your risk is very low. For further details you can consult me directly through this website.

If it has spread to involve other areas then it is likely to be stage 4 cancer. I have served in a cancer institute for years and have seen many patients like him getting chemotherapy to prolong life. Liver and kidney functions and general condition should be maintained during treatment. Radiation also helps many patients. Usually, the survival of a patient with advanced stage cancer is not more than two years with good care and treatment. This is an average and rarely patients might survive to three to five years. Chemotherapy and radiation therapy might help to extend the life of the patient and slow the disease progression but cannot cure cancer. At the age of 82 years, it is important to give him good nutrition and diet with proteins and vitamins. Pain relief is important for him and this is to be discussed with his doctor. Please see that he is comfortable. Hope this answers your question.

record.input: Hi doctor,I would like to get a second opinion on my dentist\s plan to fix my missing teeth. I am missing my upper lateral incisors since birth and have had a temporary resign bonded bridge since I was 17. I am now ready to take the step to a more permanent solution. My two options are a bridge or implants. After my X-rays and MRI scan, it became apparent that I do not have enough bone for implants and I would need a bone graft. Since I would need at least 3 mm for each side, my dentist told me that there was a chance it might not take and I would need another surgery. The position of the roots of the adjacent teeth are not a problem, there is enough space to place the implants, just not enough bone.Initially, he did say he would prefer to do the implants, since it is a better solution for long-term. When my dentist asked the opinion of another dentist in the office, he said that the bridge would be a better solution so I would not have to go through multiple surgeries and it would be done sooner. The bridge is indeed a quicker solution, but I am not very keen on having parts of my teeth removed for the bridge. It will also need replacing at some point, and if the underlying teeth have a problem, the whole bridge needs to be replaced. Both my sister and mother have a bridge (they are also missing their upper lateral incisors) and I am not impressed with either results (these were placed by different dentists).I am fine with the process taking a little longer (and being more expensive) if it means it will give me a more natural smile, but I am also afraid that I will be going through surgery for something that might not even take. My dentist now does not want to express his opinion anymore, and just keeps giving me the pros and cons. Any insights would be greatly appreciated.
record.answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. In my view, even though I do not have any pictures or X-ray, I believe it is better for you to get bridges. You can get costly Zirconium bridges which are highly aesthetic and looks exactly like natural teeth. Get those side teeth root canal treated to prevent future sensitivity or pain. This will give you a highly satisfactory result in my view. Implants need much bone support and as your lateral incisors are congenitally missing, the bone at the region will be very thin and any bone graft is bound to fail in > 50 % cases. For more details, send pictures if possible.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot provide an opinion as a human dentist might, but I can provide some information for you to consider. It is important to note that both bridges and implants have their benefits and drawbacks. A bridge fills the gap between missing teeth by attaching to the adjacent teeth, which are altered for the purpose. An implant replaces a missing tooth with an artificial replacement that is anchored in the jawbone. Implants are generally the preferred option for long-term solutions. While they may require more time and expense in the short-term, they tend to be more reliable and durable than bridges. They also do not require the adjacent teeth to be altered, which can help preserve the integrity of the remaining natural teeth. However, as you mentioned, there may not be enough bone to support the implants, and a bone graft may be necessary. While the success rate of bone grafts is high, there is always a chance of complications. On the other hand, bridges are a quicker and less expensive solution, but do require the nearby teeth to be altered. They also may need to be replaced throughout your lifetime if they fail, unlike implants, which can last for decades with proper care. However, bridges have the benefit of being a more conservative treatment that does not require invasive surgery. Ultimately, the decision of whether to pursue implants or a bridge is up to you and your dentist. It is important to carefully weigh the benefits and drawbacks of each option, as well as considering your individual dental and medical history, to make the best decision for your unique circumstances. It may also be worth seeking a second opinion from another dentist to confirm the best course of treatment.
record.answer_chatdoctor: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. I can understand your concern about your dental problems. Implantation procedure requires proper bony support for success. If there is no adequate bony support, then it may fail. So, I advise you to consult an Oral Surgeon and ask him for a detailed treatment plan. You should also discuss all the risks and benefits of both Implantation and Bridge work. Then you can decide which one suits you best. Hope this helps. Take care.

record.input: Hello doctor,After how many days of periods, sex should not happen to avoid pregnancy?
record.answer_icliniq: Hello. Your question actually pertains to a natural birth control method called rhythm method calendar method (you can search it on net). Let me tell you the safe days are the days you can actually have sex in order not to get pregnant but, this method is not full proof and there are failure rates too. Consider first day of your cycle (menstrual cycle) as the day from which you began to bleed. You keep a track of your cycles and determine your shortest and longest cycle but before that know it that your cycles must be regular. Substract 20 from your shortest cycle and 10 from your longest cycle and you get your safe days. Eg. Shortest is 26 Longest is 30. 26-20= 6 30-10= 20. Days 6 to 20 is unsafe for you. Rest all will be safe to have sex to avoid pregnancy. There are many other calculations for calendar which you yourself can search but my recommendations will be five days after the last day of your first cycle that is when the bleeding stops will be the safest (though not 100 percent).
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot have a personal opinion, but generally speaking, women have different menstrual cycles and can have ovulation occur at different times. To avoid pregnancy, it is essential to use some form of contraception method, such as condoms or hormonal birth control. Consulting with a healthcare provider can help determine the best method for an individual's specific situation.
record.answer_chatdoctor: Hi, Thanks for writing your query. I am Chat Doctor. I will try to help you in the best possible way. As per my opinion if you have regular cycles and if period is expected around 28-30 days then you can have intercourse from 10th day till 4/5 th day of next cycle(next period). This rule holds good only when there is no irregularity in menstrual cycle or any problem like POD (progesterone dominant) which can lead to pregnancy even after having unprotected sex during safe period. Hope this answers your question. Please feel free to ask for follow up queries.

Hirschsprung's disease is a rare condition that affects the large intestine, making it difficult for stool to pass through the bowel. Symptoms of Hirschsprung's disease typically include constipation, abdominal swelling, and vomiting. Diagnosis of the condition usually involves a combination of medical history, physical examination, and imaging tests such as a barium enema. However, a misdiagnosis of Hirschsprung's disease can occur since there are other causes of constipation in infants. Barium enema is a diagnostic imaging test used to examine the large intestine. During this test, a contrast material (barium) is inserted into the rectum through a tube or catheter. The barium highlights the colon and rectum on X-ray pictures. A barium enema may be used to diagnose a range of conditions affecting the colon, such as inflammation, tumors, and polyps. It is important to discuss the benefits and risks of having a barium enema with your doctor. The most important thing is to ensure that the care of your son is prioritized and you are comfortable with the course of action taken.

However, several treatments can help manage symptoms and slow the progression of the disease. The most effective way to control muscular dystrophy is by working with a team of healthcare professionals to develop a personalized treatment plan. Treatment options include: 1. Physical therapy and exercise: Regular physical therapy and exercise can help improve muscle strength and mobility. 2. Medications: Certain medications can help manage symptoms such as pain, inflammation, and muscle weakness. 3. Surgery: Surgery may be an option in some cases to improve mobility or correct orthopedic problems. 4. Assistive devices: The use of assistive devices such as braces, wheelchairs, and scooters can help individuals with muscular dystrophy remain independent and mobile. 5. Gene therapy: Some types of muscular dystrophy may be treated with gene therapy, a new and emerging technique that aims to correct the underlying genetic mutations responsible for the disease. It's important to work with a healthcare professional who specializes in muscular dystrophy to create a treatment plan that works best for you or your loved one.

record.input: Hello doctor,I am hepatitis C positive with IgM + IgG of 14.2. This test was conducted five years ago. What is the significance of this? record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot provide specific medical advice or diagnose conditions. However, I can provide you with general information about Hepatitis C and the markers IgM and IgG. Hepatitis C is a viral infection that affects the liver. IgM and IgG are immunoglobulin antibodies that are produced by the immune system in response to an infection. IgM is produced during the early stages of an infection, and IgG is produced later on, and usually stays in the body after the infection has resolved. In your case, a positive IgM indicates that you were likely infected with hepatitis C within the last 6 months before the test was taken. A positive IgG indicates that you were infected with hepatitis C at some point in the past. It is important to note that a positive test from five years ago does not necessarily mean that you are currently infected or have active hepatitis C. However, a positive test does indicate that you should continue to be monitored by a healthcare provider and potentially be tested again to determine your current status. It is also important to consider other factors such as your liver function tests (LFTs) and sonography, which you mention were normal two months ago. These tests can provide information about the health of your liver and whether or not there is currently any liver damage or inflammation. Ultimately, it is up to your healthcare provider to determine if you need further testing, monitoring, or treatment for hepatitis C. I recommend discussing your concerns and test results with your healthcare provider for personalized guidance.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 14.2 is above the cut off level for antibody detection as per the WHO criteria. It means that you can be considered as having been exposed to HCV infection. It does not mean that you have chronic active hepatitis. The other tests like viral load and genotype are required to make a diagnosis of Hepatitis C infection. You should consult an infectious disease specialist who will advise further tests and management if needed.

record.input: Hi doctor,I am a 26 year old married woman. I have missed my period. When I tested using a Prega News kit, the result was positive. Do I need to take any other medicine with MTP? Please tell me in detail. I do not like to go hospital for this. Is it safe to get it done at home?
record.answer_icliniq: Hello. Firstly, your decision of not reporting to the doctor itself is very wrong and trying to abort at home is further wrong as MTP can lead to a lot of bleeding complications which you cannot manage at home after taking the medicine as a dosed protocol. The MTP kit (medical termination of pregnancy) is available in the market as unwanted, but the instruction to take the medication is not accurate for requirement of dose depends on the period of gestation first. Hence, it is necessary that you should undergo an ultrasound pelvis to locate the pregnancy first whether it is intrauterine or ectopic. Once intrauterine confirmed, then the tablets can be taken in a scheduled manner under your local gynecologist supervision in a sequential manner with Mifepristone first and then Misoprostol. Sometimes, Misoprostol has to be continued till 2000 microgram maximum dosage in case of failed abortion with the initial dosage, which may lead to surgical abortion or D and C (dilatation and curettage). So, it is recommended that you should meet your gynecologist first and do not attempt any home abortion. It is not safe at all. Take care.

We asked for the report and they told its volume is around 385. Now they are telling, we have to free my sample and need to wait until my ovary size becomes normal. They explained that it might be dangerous to the mother if I get pregnant. I am a little bit confused now. What should I do? I have done all the precautions which they explained. I took leave for 20 days to complete this cycle with success. But at last the hospital is telling we have to wait and need to start a new cycle. IVF cycle cost is really high. So please guide me what should I do? Should I take the risk of waiting until my ovary becomes normal?

Presently they cannot be used as hyperstimulation syn

record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot make any diagnosis or treatment recommendation. Anterior urethral strictures can cause a variety of urinary symptoms such as weak urine flow, frequent urination, and sensation of incomplete bladder emptying. The severity of symptoms can vary depending on the degree of the stricture. When the stricture is less than 30%, some doctors may recommend monitoring without any intervention, and it is possible that a dilation may not be strictly necessary. However, even though your urinary symptoms are currently mild, urethral strictures are known to progress and might potentially lead to more complicated problems. Furthermore, the presence of post-void dribbling could indicate some level of obstruction that could worsen the stricture in time. In your case, if your urologist is recommending a repeat cystoscopy with dilation, it could be for the purpose of trying to prevent the stricture from progressing or worsening over time. However, it's essential to have a good conversation with your urologist to understand and discuss the significance of having a dilation done and the potential benefits and risks involved. It's also essential to note that urethral dilation is a temporary measure, and the stricture can recur. In some cases, recurrent dilations may not be possible, and other treatment options such as urethroplasty may need to be considered. In summary, I recommend discussing your concerns and your specific medical history with your urologist. They will be able to provide personalized advice and guide you in making the decision that is best suited for your condition.

What I mean is most of the wisdom teeth are removed not to create space but it does not serve the purpose to chew well instead of harm the surrounding gums. There is a rule that the molars have to hit each other at certain points only then it helps you in chewing your food efficiently. Hence, there is no harm in pulling out the premolars in order to gain space, because there would not be any gap left behind after the treatment is completed. So, I advise you not to panic because you are losing your premolar tooth but that is definitely going to help the treatment become a success. Take care.

record.input: Hello doctor,Are there chances of developing chickenpox after shingles?
record.answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. Chickenpox and shingles are caused due to the same virus. Chickenpox occurs first and the virus stays dormant in the dorsal ganglia of the spinal nerves. After years or when immunity decreases, the person gets an attack of shingles. Most people get shingles only once, but few can get 2 to 3 times. After shingles no one gets chickenpox.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot be a doctor and provide medical advice to patients. However, I can inform you that shingles is caused by the reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus (VZV) that causes chickenpox. If you have had chickenpox before, the virus remains dormant in your body and can reactivate later in life, causing shingles. It is possible to develop chickenpox after shingles if you have not had chickenpox previously or if you have a weakened immune system. If you have symptoms of chickenpox or shingles, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider for an accurate diagnosis and appropriate treatment.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 100% yes. Shingles is caused by varicella zoster virus (VZV) which is also responsible for causing Chicken pox. So if someone has had chickenpox in their past they can develop shingles again. There are two types of shingles: herpes zoster and postherpetic neuralgia. Herpes zoster is an acute viral infection that usually lasts 2-3 weeks. Postherpetic neuralgia is a chronic pain condition that occurs after healing of the skin lesions. It is estimated that approximately 15 to 20 percent of people with shingles will develop postherpetic neuralgia.

Regular medication includes tablet Ciplar LA (Propranolol) 10 mg once a day in the morning after breakfast. Before starting this, your resting pulse rate should be more than 65 per minute or get it prescribed from a local doctor. This will one to two to acts and gradually increase the dose. record.input: Hello doctor,I have a question to ask about the ovulation period of a woman. How can we count the day of ovulation? Do we count from the first day of the period or from the last day of period?
record.answer_icliniq: Hello. The ovulation day is counted backward from the last day of the menstrual cycle. For example, if a woman had a regular cycle of 30 days so she will ovulate 14 days prior to her last day of that cycle. Meaning, she ovulates on the 16th day of her cycle. Another example is that if a lady has a regular cycle of 28 days then her ovulation will occur 14 days prior to her last day. Meaning, she will ovulate on the 14th day of her cycle. It is so because the menses start exactly after 14 days of ovulation. For more information consult a sexologist online
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I don't hold specialty in medical field, but I can still answer your question. Typically, a woman's ovulation period occurs around 14 days before their next menstrual cycle is scheduled to begin. So, if a woman has a regular 28-day menstrual cycle, she is likely to ovulate on day 14 (counting from the first day of her last period). However, this can vary from woman to woman and may not be accurate for individuals with irregular menstrual cycles. To accurately determine your ovulation period, it is recommended to track your menstrual cycle and other ovulation symptoms over a few months, or to use ovulation prediction kits.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1st day of period is not the first day of ovulation. Ovulation starts from the end of menstrual cycle and continues for next 2 weeks. So if your periods are regular with 28 days duration then ovulation will be around 14th day. Nowadays you can find many ovulation calculator apps in which you just need to enter your date of period and date of next expected period and it will give you exact day of ovulation.

record.answer_chatgpt: Hi! As an AI language model, I'm not a licensed healthcare provider and cannot offer medical advice. However, I will provide you with general information that might be helpful: Feeling nauseous after a workout can happen for many reasons. Intense exercise can cause your blood vessels to expand, your blood pressure to drop, and your stomach to feel queasy. Additionally, dehydration, low blood sugar, and even anxiety can lead to feelings of nausea after working out. If you regularly feel nauseous after working out, it's important to speak with a healthcare professional to determine the underlying cause. To prevent nausea before a workout, you may want to avoid eating large meals or foods that are high in fat or fiber, which can slow down digestion. Instead, opt for a light snack high in carbohydrates and low in fat before your workout, such as a banana or a piece of toast with honey. During your workout, it's important to stay hydrated by drinking water or a sports drink. It might also be helpful to take frequent breaks to let your body rest and recover. After your workout, cool down with gentle stretches to help your body gradually return to its resting state. You can also refuel with a snack or meal that is high in carbohydrates and protein, such as a smoothie with Greek yogurt and berries. High-intensity workouts, such as HIIT (high-intensity interval training), are more likely to cause nausea because they require a lot of physical exertion. However, this doesn't mean you should avoid them altogether. If you experience nausea during a workout, take a break, rest, and wait for the symptoms to pass. Finally, foods that are high in electrolytes, such as bananas or coconut water, can help prevent nausea by replenishing lost fluids and minerals during your workout.

record.input: Hello doctor,I want to seal my semen and ejaculation pathway to preserve my semen in the body permanently. Please help.

record.input: Hi doctor,I am here to ask some questions regarding HIV and some other possible STDs for the woman having unprotected oral sex (cunnilingus). record.answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. Please say whether the throat pain got resolved with medications. If you have it, then it is unlikely to be a cause due to HIV infection. Secondly, the home kit for HIV has a low sensitivity and low specificity. So, it is inconclusive. ELISA (enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay) is a standard test advisable for HIV testing. Since it is four months, the window period for acquiring an HIV infection has overlapped, and hence, having a negative report at this point is very sure to conclude as not having HIV infection. I would suggest you get tested for ELISA now and repeat it after four weeks again or after a month. Also get a swab sampling done for your throat pain and consult a physician.
record.answer_chatgpt: Hello, as an AI language model, I am not a licensed doctor and cannot provide a medical diagnosis. However, I can provide you with some information that may be helpful. First of all, a HIV rapid screen test taken 12 weeks and 2 days after exposure is considered to be highly accurate, and a negative result at this time is usually conclusive. However, the window period for HIV testing can vary depending on different factors, so it is good practice to follow up with another test at six months after exposure to confirm the negative result. Regarding the accuracy of home testing, while some self-testing kits have been approved by FDA for sale in the US and are considered to be reliable, it is still recommended to confirm the result with a healthcare provider. Regarding your symptoms, small red bumps at the back of the throat can be caused by a variety of factors, including strep throat or pharyngitis. It is difficult to determine whether the symptoms are related to STDs or not since many STDs can present with no symptoms or different symptoms, and some other factors such as allergies or infection can cause similar symptoms. It is always important to have any concerning symptoms checked with a healthcare provider to receive proper diagnosis and treatment. Lastly, some STDs can present with symptoms months after exposure, while others have no symptoms at all. Therefore, it is important to practice safe sex and get tested for STDs regularly, especially if you have engaged in unprotected sexual activities.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1. You should not rely on self-test kits as they may be wrong, but if you are sure about your partner being negative then this is a good sign. 2. The redness in throat may just be due to stress or anxiety. There is nothing to worry about. 3. record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I do not have personal experiences, but I can elaborate on the topic. The menstrual cycle affects the brain in various ways. There are changes in hormone levels, such as estrogen and progesterone, which can affect mood, cognition, and behavior. Some of the fascinating things that happen to the brain during the menstrual cycle include: 1. Changes in brain structure: Studies have shown that the brain's gray matter volume changes during different phases of the menstrual cycle. Specifically, the hippocampus, which is involved in memory and spatial navigation, shows structural changes in response to hormonal fluctuations. 2. Changes in emotional processing: Hormonal changes during the menstrual cycle can affect emotional processing, leading to changes in mood, anxiety, and irritability. This is because estrogen and progesterone impact the levels of neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, which are involved in mood regulation. 3. Changes in cognition: Studies have found that cognitive performance, such as attention, memory, and executive function, can vary across different phases of the menstrual cycle. This variability may be due to changes in brain chemistry and hormonal fluctuations. The brain responds to a woman's menstrual cycle mainly through the release of hormones. The hypothalamus in the brain regulates the release of hormones from the pituitary gland, which ultimately affects the ovaries to produce estrogen and progesterone. These hormones then have different effects on the brain, influencing various aspects of cognition, mood, and behavior. Additionally, other brain regions, such as the amygdala and prefrontal cortex, are also involved in the complex interplay between hormones and cognitive-emotional processing during the menstrual cycle.

You can still enjoy a mug of coffee every now and then during your pregnancy. That is about two mugs of instant coffee or one mug of filter coffee. If caffeine intake is more than 200 mg per day, there are chances of low birth weight. Take care.
record.answer_chatgpt: As an AI language model, I cannot give medical advice or consult on personal health matters. However, I can provide you with some information that may be helpful. It is generally safe for pregnant women to consume moderate amounts of caffeine, which means up to 200 milligrams per day or about one 12-ounce cup of coffee. However, it is important to note that high doses of caffeine may increase the risk of miscarriage, preterm birth, low birth weight, and other pregnancy complications. It is recommended that you discuss caffeine consumption with your healthcare provider to determine what is best for you and your baby during pregnancy.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1. Yes, caffeine is safe in small amounts during pregnancy2. Caffeine is found in many foods and beverages including coffee, tea, cola, chocolate, energy drinks, painkillers and some medicines3. Small amounts of caffeine (up to 200 mg per day) do not seem to harm the baby4. However, larger amounts can cause problems such as low birth weight, irritability, sleep disturbances, poor feeding habits, increased risk for preterm delivery and anemia5. The safest way to limit caffeine intake is to avoid all products containing caffeine6.

record.input: Hello doctor,A year ago, I was diagnosed with Emphysema, and before four years, I was diagnosed with bronchitis, but after six months, I had a warm feeling in my stomach, followed by nausea. record.answer_icliniq: Hello. No, you need not go to the ER. The burning sensation in the stomach and nausea you are having is not related to your emphysema and bronchitis. It is actually due to the antibiotics and other medicines you are taking because antibiotics cause secretion of an excessive amount of acid in the stomach, which leads to a feeling of burning and nausea. The treatment of your problem is simple, take these medicines along with antibiotics to prevent excess acid formation. (1) Capsule Rabeprazole two times a day. (2) Tablet Rantac (Ranitidine) two times a day. (3) Tablet Domperidone 10 mg two times a day.

If your symptoms are mild and you are not experiencing any difficulty breathing, you may want to consider waiting a short period of time to see if your symptoms improve. However, if your symptoms worsen or you have trouble breathing, seek emergency medical attention immediately. record.input: Hi doctor,I am having type 2 uncontrolled diabetes. My endocrinologist is asking me to undergo a bariatric surgery. Kindly advise. Can you explain if it is required and how is it going to help control my diabetes and hypertension?
record.answer_icliniq: Hi. Bariatric or metabolic surgeries help to achieve long term remission from type 2 diabetes. These are scientifically proven, very safe and highly effective. They result in long term weight loss in individuals suffering from an excess weight problem, and also lead to long term remission from co-morbid medical conditions such as high BP and sleep apnea. Bariatric or metabolic surgeries alter hormones which control body fat stores and reset set value for fat storage to a lower level. Same hormonal changes are responsible for remission from problems like diabetes. Depending upon your weight, presence or absence of other medical problems, I choose different surgeries. Sleeve with bypass combination surgeries is more powerful and durable.

Bariatric surgery can improve diabetes control by decreasing insulin resistance and improving glucose metabolism. Additionally, weight loss resulting from the surgery can decrease blood pressure, improve lipid profile, and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. It is important to discuss with your endocrinologist and a qualified bariatric surgeon to determine if this procedure is appropriate for you, the risks and benefits, and the potential outcomes.

record.answer_chatgpt: Hello, as an AI language model, I am not capable of providing medical diagnoses or suggestions. However, I can provide general information on subserosal fibroids. Subserosal fibroids are fibroids that grow outside of the uterus and therefore, typically do not cause major symptoms. The decision to remove a subserosal fibroid depends on the size, location, and symptoms it is causing. If the fibroid is large and causing symptoms such as pain, heavy bleeding or pressure on nearby organs, surgery may be recommended. Regarding the type of fibroid you have mentioned, myxoid necrobiosis, this condition is not typically a concern but should be monitored by your doctor. Depending on the severity of the necrobiosis, your doctor may recommend periodic monitoring or surgery to remove the fibroid. The risks associated with fibroid surgery depend on the type of surgery chosen. If you opt for minimally invasive surgery, such as laparoscopic or robotic surgery, risks are relatively low. However, traditional surgery, such as a myomectomy or hysterectomy, carries a higher risk of infection and complications. If you plan to get pregnant, it is important to consult your doctor about the effect your fibroid may have on fertility and pregnancy. A subserosal fibroid typically does not affect fertility, but its size and location can impact the pregnancy, such as the likelihood of miscarriage or preterm birth. Overall, I recommend that you consult with your gynecologist to decide on the best course of action for your individual situation.
record.answer_chatdoctor: 1) Yes, the fibroid needs to be removed as it may cause complications like bleeding, pain etc. 2) The surgery has no risk of affecting the uterus but there is always possibility of recurrence after removal of fibroid due to its benign nature. 3) It is not advisable to have pregnancy with such a large fibroid.

However, I can provide you with some general information regarding pregnancy blood tests. Blood tests to confirm pregnancy usually measure the levels of human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG) in your blood. If your hCG levels are elevated, it can confirm pregnancy. HCG levels typically double every 2 to 3 days in the first weeks of pregnancy. Based on the hCG level, your doctor can estimate how far along you are in your pregnancy. In addition to hCG, blood tests can also check other hormones such as progesterone, estrogen, and thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH). These tests can help your doctor monitor your pregnancy and ensure your hormone levels are within the normal range. It is recommended that you follow your doctor's advice regarding additional blood tests or checks, as every pregnancy is different and may require different testing. Your doctor may recommend tests to check for genetic abnormalities, sexually transmitted diseases, or other conditions that could affect your pregnancy. If you have any concerns regarding your blood test results or your pregnancy, it is best to talk to your doctor. They will be able to provide you with specific information and answer any questions you may have.
